categories.title: Dua Before Sleeping and Upon Waking Up

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Ayatul Kursi
dua.dua_in_arabic:
بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْـمِ
اَللّٰهُ لَآ اِلٰهَ اِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّوْمُ ۚ لَا تَأْخُذُهٗ سِنَةٌ وَّلَا نَوْمٌ ۚ لَّهٗ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْاَرْضِ ۗ مَنْ ذَا الَّذِيْ يَشْفَعُ عِندَهٗ اِلَّا بِاِذْنِهٖ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ اَيْدِيْهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ۖ وَلَا يُحِيْطُوْنَ بِشَيْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهٖ اِلَّا بِمَا شَآءَ ۚ وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْاَرْضَ ۖ وَلَا يَئُوْدُهٗ حِفْظُهُمَا ۚ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْعَظِيْمُ  ؁٢٥٥
dua.bangla_translation:
In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

2:255 Allah - there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of existence. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Who is it that can intercede with Him except by His permission? He knows what is before them and what will be after them, and they encompass not a thing of His knowledge except for what He wills. His Kursi extends over the heavens and the earth, and their preservation tires Him not. And He is the Most High, the Most Great.
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Abu Hurairah (RA): The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) appointed me to guard the store of Zakat during Ramadan. Someone came and started taking handfuls of the food. I caught him and said, "I will take you to Allah's Messenger." ... He told me, "When you go to bed, recite Ayat al-Kursi, for then a guardian from Allah will keep protecting you, and Satan will not approach you till dawn." The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "He told you the truth, although he is a liar; that was Satan."

Sahih al-Bukhari 5010

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Surah Al-Kafirun
dua.dua_in_arabic:
بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ‏
قُلۡ يٰۤاَيُّهَا الۡكٰفِرُوۡنَۙ ‏﴿﻿۱﻿﴾  لَاۤ اَعۡبُدُ مَا تَعۡبُدُوۡنَۙ‏ ﴿﻿۲﻿﴾  وَلَاۤ اَنۡـتُمۡ عٰبِدُوۡنَ مَاۤ اَعۡبُدُ‌ ۚ‏ ﴿﻿۳﻿﴾  وَلَاۤ اَنَا عَابِدٌ مَّا عَبَدۡتُّمۡۙ‏ ﴿﻿۴﻿﴾  وَ لَاۤ اَنۡـتُمۡ عٰبِدُوۡنَ مَاۤ اَعۡبُدُ ؕ‏ ﴿﻿۵﻿﴾  لَـكُمۡ دِيۡنُكُمۡ وَلِىَ دِيۡنِ ﴿﻿۶﻿﴾
dua.dua_in_bangla:
বিসমিল্লাহ হির রহমানের রাহীম
১ কুল ইয়া আইয়ুহাল কাফিরুন,
২ লা আ'বুদু মা তা'বুদুন।
৩ ওয়ালা আন্তুম 'আবিদুনা মা আ'বুদ
৪ ওয়ালা আনা 'আবিদুম মা 'আবাদতুম।
৫ ওয়ালা আন্তুম 'আবিদুনা মা আ'বুদ।
৬ লাকুম দীনুকুম ওয়ালিয়া দ্বীন।
dua.bangla_translation:
In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful
1. Say, O disbelievers,
2. I do not worship what you worship.
3. Nor are you worshippers of what I worship.
4. Nor will I be a worshipper of what you worship.
5. Nor will you be worshippers of what I worship.
6. For you is your religion, and for me is my religion.
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Farwah ibn Nawfal (RA) from his father: The Prophet (peace be upon him) told Nawfal, "Recite 'Say, O disbelievers' [Surah Al-Kafirun] before you sleep, for it is a declaration of freedom from shirk."

Sunan Abi Dawud 5055

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Surah Al-Ikhlas
dua.dua_in_arabic:
بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْمِ،
 قُلْ هُوَ اللّٰهُ اَحَدٌ ﴿١﴾ اَللّٰهُ الصَّمَدُ ﴿٢﴾ لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُوْلَدْ ﴿٣﴾ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهٗ كُفُوًا اَحَدٌ {٤}
dua.dua_in_bangla:
বিসমিল্লাহির রাহমানির রাহিম

১: কুল হুওয়াল্লা হু আহাদ,
২: আল্লা হুসসামাদ,
৩: লাম ইয়ালিদ ওয়ালাম ইউলাদ,
৪: ওয়ালাম ইয়াকুল্লাহু কুফুওয়ান আহাদ।
dua.bangla_translation:
In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

1. Say, He is Allah, [who is] One,
2. Allah, the Eternal Refuge.
3. He neither begets nor is born,
4. Nor is there to Him any equivalent.
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Aisha (RA): Every night when the Prophet (peace be upon him) went to bed, he would recite Surah Al-Ikhlas, Surah Al-Falaq, and Surah An-Nas, then cup his hands together, blow into them, and wipe as much of his body as he could reach, starting with his head and face and the front of his body, doing this three times.

Sahih al-Bukhari 5017

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Surah Al-Falaq
dua.dua_in_arabic:
بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْـمِ
 قُلْ اَعُوْذُ بِرَبِّ الْفَلَقِ ﴿١﴾ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ ﴿٢﴾ وَمِنْ شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ اِذَا وَقَبَ ﴿٣﴾ وَمِنْ شَرِّ النَّفَّاثَاتِ فِي الْعُقَدِ ﴿٤﴾ وَمِنْ شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ اِذَا حَسَدَ {٥}
dua.dua_in_bangla:
বিসমিল্লাহির রাহমানির রাহিম

১: কুল্ আ‘ঊযু বিরব্বিল্ ফালাক্বি,
২: মিন্ শার রিমা-খলাক্ব,
৩: অমিন্ শাররি গ-সিক্বিন্ ইযা-অক্বাব্,
৪: অমিন্ শাররি ন্নাফ্ফা-ছা-তি ফিল্ ‘উক্বদ্,
৫: মিন্ শাররি হা-সিদিন্ ইযা-হাসাদ্।
dua.bangla_translation:
In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

1. Say, I seek refuge in the Lord of daybreak,
2. From the evil of that which He created,
3. And from the evil of darkness when it settles,
4. And from the evil of the blowers in knots [i.e. practitioners of magic],
5. And from the evil of an envier when he envies.
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Aisha (RA): Every night when the Prophet (peace be upon him) went to bed, he would recite Surah Al-Ikhlas, Surah Al-Falaq, and Surah An-Nas, then cup his hands together, blow into them, and wipe as much of his body as he could reach, starting with his head and face and the front of his body, doing this three times.

Sahih al-Bukhari 5017

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Surah An-Nas
dua.dua_in_arabic:
بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْـمِ 
 قُلْ اَعُوْذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ ﴿١﴾ مَلِكِ النَّاسِ ﴿٢﴾ اِلٰهِ النَّاسِ ﴿٣﴾ مِنْ شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ ﴿٤﴾ الَّذِيْ يُوَسْوِسُ فِيْ صُدُوْرِ النَّاسِ ﴿٥﴾ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ {٦}
dua.dua_in_bangla:
বিসমিল্লাহির রাহমানির রাহিম

১: কুল আউযু বিরাব্বিন নাস,
২: মালিকিন্ নাস,
৩: ইলাহিন্ নাস,
৪: মিন্ শররিল ওয়াস্ ওয়াসিল খান্নাস,
৫: আল্লাযী ইউওযাসবিসু ফী ছুদুরিন্নাস,
৬: মিনা জিন্নাতি ওয়ান্নাস।
dua.bangla_translation:
In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

1. Say, I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind,
2. The Sovereign of mankind,
3. The God of mankind,
4. From the evil of the retreating whisperer -
5. Who whispers [evil] into the breasts of mankind -
6. From among the jinn and mankind.
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Aisha (RA): Every night when the Prophet (peace be upon him) went to bed, he would recite Surah Al-Ikhlas, Surah Al-Falaq, and Surah An-Nas, then cup his hands together, blow into them, and wipe as much of his body as he could reach, starting with his head and face and the front of his body, doing this three times.

Sahih al-Bukhari 5017

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Surah Al-Baqarah, Last 2 Ayahs
dua.dua_in_arabic:
بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْـمِ 
آمَنَ الرَّسُولُ بِمَآ أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِن رَّبِّهِ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ ۚ كُلٌّ آمَنَ بِاللَّـهِ وَمَلَائِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ لَا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِّن رُّسُلِهِ ۚ وَقَالُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا ۖ غُفْرَانَكَ رَبَّنَا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ ﴿٢٨٥﴾ لَا يُكَلِّفُ اللَّـهُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا ۚ لَهَا مَا كَسَبَتْ وَعَلَيْهَا مَا اكْتَسَبَتْ ۗ رَبَّنَا لَا تُؤَاخِذْنَا إِن نَّسِينَا أَوْ أَخْطَأْنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا وَلَا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنَا إِصْرًا كَمَا حَمَلْتَهُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا وَلَا تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِ ۖ وَاعْفُ عَنَّا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَا ۚ أَنْتَ مَوْلَانَا فَانْصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ ﴿٢٨٦﴾
dua.dua_in_bangla:
বিসমিল্লাহির রাহমানির রাহিম
২:২৮৫ আমানার্‌ রাসূলু বিমা উন্‌যিলা ইলাইহি মির্‌ রাব্বিহি ওয়াল্‌মু’মিনূন। কুল্লুন্‌ আমান বিল্লাহি ওয়া মালাইিকাতিহি ওয়া কুতুবিহি ওয়া রুসুলিহি লা নুফার্‌রিকু বাইনা আহাদিম্‌ মির্‌ রুসুলিহি, ওয়া ক্বালু সামি’না ওয়া আত্বা’না গুফ্‌রানাকা রাব্বানা ওয়া ইলাইকাল্‌ মাসীর।
২:২৮৬ লা ইউকাল্লিফুল্লাহু নাফ্‌সান্‌ ইল্লা উস্‌’আহা, লাহা মা কাসাবাত্‌ ওয়া আলাইহা মা কাসাবাত্‌। রাব্বানা লা তু’আখিজনা ইন্‌ নাসিনা আও আখ্‌তা’না, রাব্বানা ওয়া লা তাহ্‌মিল্‌ আলাইনা ইস্‌রান্‌ কামা হামাল্‌তাহু আলাল্লধিনা মিন্‌ ক্বাবলিনা, রাব্বানা ওয়া লা তুহাম্‌মিল্‌না মা লা ত্বাক্বতালানা বিহি, ওয়া’ফু আন্না ওয়া গ্‌ফির্‌ লানা ওয়া রাহ্‌মনা, আন্‌তা মাওলানা ফান্‌সুরনা আলাল্‌ ক্বাওমিল্‌ কাফিরীন।
dua.bangla_translation:
In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

2:285 The Messenger has believed in what was revealed to him from his Lord, and [so have] the believers. All of them have believed in Allah and His angels and His books and His messengers, [saying], "We make no distinction between any of His messengers." And they say, "We hear and we obey. [We seek] Your forgiveness, our Lord, and to You is the [final] destination."
2:286 Allah does not charge a soul except with that within its capacity. It will have [the consequence of] what [good] it has gained, and it will bear [the consequence of] what [evil] it has earned. "Our Lord, do not impose blame upon us if we forget or make a mistake. Our Lord, and lay not upon us a burden like that which You laid upon those before us. Our Lord, and burden us not with that which we have no ability to bear. And pardon us; and forgive us; and have mercy upon us. You are our protector, so give us victory over the disbelieving people."
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Abu Mas'ud Al-Ansari (RA): The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "Whoever recites the last two verses of Surah Al-Baqarah at night, they will be sufficient for him."

Sahih al-Bukhari 5040

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Surah As-Sajdah
dua.dua_in_arabic:
بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْـمِ

(1) الٓمٓ

(2) تَنزِيلُ ٱلۡكِتَٰبِ لَا رَيۡبَ فِيهِ مِن رَّبِّ ٱلۡعَٰلَمِينَ

(3) أَمۡ يَقُولُونَ ٱفۡتَرَٮٰهُ‌ۚ بَلۡ هُوَ ٱلۡحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ لِتُنذِرَ قَوۡمًا مَّآ أَتَٰهُم مِّن نَّذِيرٍ مِّن قَبۡلِكَ لَعَلَّهُمۡ يَهۡتَدُونَ

(4) ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا فِى سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ ٱسۡتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلۡعَرۡشِ‌ۖ مَا لَكُم مِّن دُونِهِۦ مِن وَلِىٍّ وَلَا شَفِيعٍ‌ۚ أَفَلَا تَتَذَكَّرُونَ

(5) يُدَبِّرُ ٱلۡأَمۡرَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ إِلَى ٱلۡأَرۡضِ ثُمَّ يَعۡرُجُ إِلَيۡهِ فِى يَوۡمٍ كَانَ مِقۡدَارُهُۥٓ أَلۡفَ سَنَةٍ مِّمَّا تَعُدُّونَ

(6) ذَٰلِكَ عَٰلِمُ ٱلۡغَيۡبِ وَٱلشَّهَٰدَةِ ٱلۡعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ

(7) ٱلَّذِىٓ أَحۡسَنَ كُلَّ شَىۡءٍ خَلَقَهُۥ‌ۖ وَبَدَأَ خَلۡقَ ٱلۡإِنسَٰنِ مِن طِينٍ

(8) ثُمَّ جَعَلَ نَسۡلَهُۥ مِن سُلَٰلَةٍ مِّن مَّآءٍ مَّهِينٍ

(9) ثُمَّ سَوَّٮٰهُ وَنَفَخَ فِيهِ مِن رُّوحِهِۦ‌ۖ وَجَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلسَّمۡعَ وَٱلۡأَبۡصَٰرَ وَٱلۡأَفۡــِٔدَةَ‌ۚ قَلِيلاً مَّا تَشۡكُرُونَ

(10) وَقَالُوٓاۡ أَءِذَا ضَلَلۡنَا فِى ٱلۡأَرۡضِ أَءِنَّا لَفِى خَلۡقٍ جَدِيدِۭ‌ۚ بَلۡ هُم بِلِقَآءِ رَبِّهِمۡ كَٰفِرُونَ

(11) قُلۡ يَتَوَفَّٰكُم مَّلَكُ ٱلۡمَوۡتِ ٱلَّذِى وُكِّلَ بِكُمۡ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُمۡ تُرۡجَعُونَ

(12) وَلَوۡ تَرَىٰٓ إِذِ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمُونَ نَاكِسُواۡ رُءُوسِهِمۡ عِندَ رَبِّهِمۡ رَبَّنَآ أَبۡصَرۡنَا وَسَمِعۡنَا فَٱرۡجِعۡنَا نَعۡمَلۡ صَٰلِحًا إِنَّا مُوقِنُونَ

(13) وَلَوۡ شِئۡنَا لَأَتَيۡنَا كُلَّ نَفۡسٍ هُدَٮٰهَا وَلَٰكِنۡ حَقَّ ٱلۡقَوۡلُ مِنِّى لَأَمۡلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنَ ٱلۡجِنَّةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ أَجۡمَعِينَ

(14) فَذُوقُواۡ بِمَا نَسِيتُمۡ لِقَآءَ يَوۡمِكُمۡ هَٰذَآ إِنَّا نَسِينَٰكُمۡ‌ۖ وَذُوقُواۡ عَذَابَ ٱلۡخُلۡدِ بِمَا كُنتُمۡ تَعۡمَلُونَ

(15) إِنَّمَا يُؤۡمِنُ بِـَٔـايَٰتِنَا ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِّرُواۡ بِهَا خَرُّواۡ سُجَّدًا وَسَبَّحُواۡ بِحَمۡدِ رَبِّهِمۡ وَهُمۡ لَا يَسۡتَكۡبِرُونَ

(16) تَتَجَافَىٰ جُنُوبُهُمۡ عَنِ ٱلۡمَضَاجِعِ يَدۡعُونَ رَبَّهُمۡ خَوۡفًا وَطَمَعًا وَمِمَّا رَزَقۡنَٰهُمۡ يُنفِقُونَ

(17) فَلَا تَعۡلَمُ نَفۡسٌ مَّآ أُخۡفِىَ لَهُم مِّن قُرَّةِ أَعۡيُنٍ جَزَآءَۢ بِمَا كَانُواۡ يَعۡمَلُونَ

(18) أَفَمَن كَانَ مُؤۡمِنًا كَمَن كَانَ فَاسِقًا‌ۚ لَّا يَسۡتَوُۥنَ

(19) أَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواۡ وَعَمِلُواۡ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ فَلَهُمۡ جَنَّٰتُ ٱلۡمَأۡوَىٰ نُزُلاَۢ بِمَا كَانُواۡ يَعۡمَلُونَ

(20) وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ فَسَقُواۡ فَمَأۡوَٮٰهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ‌ۖ كُلَّمَآ أَرَادُوٓاۡ أَن يَخۡرُجُواۡ مِنۡهَآ أُعِيدُواۡ فِيهَا وَقِيلَ لَهُمۡ ذُوقُواۡ عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تُكَذِّبُونَ

(21) وَلَنُذِيقَنَّهُم مِّنَ ٱلۡعَذَابِ ٱلۡأَدۡنَىٰ دُونَ ٱلۡعَذَابِ ٱلۡأَكۡبَرِ لَعَلَّهُمۡ يَرۡجِعُونَ

(22) وَمَنۡ أَظۡلَمُ مِمَّن ذُكِّرَ بِـَٔـايَٰتِ رَبِّهِۦ ثُمَّ أَعۡرَضَ عَنۡهَآ‌ۚ إِنَّا مِنَ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِينَ مُنتَقِمُونَ

(23) وَلَقَدۡ ءَاتَيۡنَا مُوسَى ٱلۡكِتَٰبَ فَلَا تَكُن فِى مِرۡيَةٍ مِّن لِّقَآئِهِۦ‌ۖ وَجَعَلۡنَٰهُ هُدًى لِّبَنِىٓ إِسۡرَٰٓءِيلَ

(24) وَجَعَلۡنَا مِنۡهُمۡ أَئِمَّةً يَهۡدُونَ بِأَمۡرِنَا لَمَّا صَبَرُواۡ‌ۖ وَكَانُواۡ بِـَٔـايَٰتِنَا يُوقِنُونَ

(25) إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ يَفۡصِلُ بَيۡنَهُمۡ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ فِيمَا كَانُواۡ فِيهِ يَخۡتَلِفُونَ

(26) أَوَلَمۡ يَهۡدِ لَهُمۡ كَمۡ أَهۡلَكۡنَا مِن قَبۡلِهِم مِّنَ ٱلۡقُرُونِ يَمۡشُونَ فِى مَسَٰكِنِهِمۡ‌ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَأَيَٰتٍ‌ۖ أَفَلَا يَسۡمَعُونَ

(27) أَوَلَمۡ يَرَوۡاۡ أَنَّا نَسُوقُ ٱلۡمَآءَ إِلَى ٱلۡأَرۡضِ ٱلۡجُرُزِ فَنُخۡرِجُ بِهِۦ زَرۡعًا تَأۡكُلُ مِنۡهُ أَنۡعَٰمُهُمۡ وَأَنفُسُهُمۡ‌ۖ أَفَلَا يُبۡصِرُونَ

(28) وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَٰذَا ٱلۡفَتۡحُ إِن كُنتُمۡ صَٰدِقِينَ

(29) قُلۡ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡفَتۡحِ لَا يَنفَعُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓاۡ إِيمَٰنُهُمۡ وَلَا هُمۡ يُنظَرُونَ

(30) فَأَعۡرِضۡ عَنۡهُمۡ وَٱنتَظِرۡ إِنَّهُم مُّنتَظِرُونَ
dua.bangla_translation:
In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
Alif, Lam, Meem.

[This is] the revelation of the Book about which there is no doubt, from the Lord of the worlds.

Or do they say, "He invented it"? Rather, it is the truth from your Lord, that you may warn a people to whom no warner has come before you, so perhaps they will be guided.

Allah is the one who created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six days; then He established Himself above the Throne. You have not besides Him any protector or any intercessor; so will you not be reminded?

He arranges [each] matter from the heaven to the earth; then it will ascend to Him in a Day, the extent of which is a thousand years of those which you count.

That is the Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, the Exalted in Might, the Merciful,

Who perfected everything which He created and began the creation of man from clay.

Then He made his posterity out of the extract of a fluid disdained.

Then He proportioned him and breathed into him from His [created] soul and made for you hearing and vision and hearts; little are you grateful.

And they say, "When we are lost within the earth, will we indeed be [recreated] in a new creation?" Rather, they deny the meeting with their Lord.

Say, "The angel of death who has been entrusted with you will take you. Then to your Lord you will be returned."

If you could but see when the criminals are hanging their heads before their Lord, [saying], "Our Lord, we have seen and heard, so return us [to the world]; we will do righteousness. Indeed, we are now certain."

And if We had willed, We could have given every soul its guidance, but the word from Me will come into effect that I will surely fill Hell with jinn and people all together.

So taste [the punishment] because you forgot the meeting of this, your Day; indeed, We have forgotten you. And taste the punishment of eternity for what you used to do.

Only those believe in Our verses who, when they are reminded by them, fall down in prostration and glorify their Lord with praise, and they are not arrogant.

Their sides part from [their] beds; they call upon their Lord in fear and hope, and from what We have provided them, they spend.

And no soul knows what has been hidden for them of comfort for eyes as reward for what they used to do.

Then is one who was a believer like one who was defiantly disobedient? They are not equal.

As for those who believed and did righteous deeds, for them will be gardens of dwelling, as hospitality for what they used to do.

But as for those who defiantly disobeyed, their refuge is the Fire. Every time they wish to emerge from it, they will be returned to it, and it will be said to them, "Taste the punishment of the Fire which you used to deny."

And We will surely let them taste the nearer punishment before the greater punishment, so that perhaps they will return.

And who is more unjust than one who is reminded of the verses of his Lord, then turns away from them? Indeed, We will take retribution from the criminals.

And We certainly gave Moses the Scripture, so do not be in doubt over meeting him. And We made it a guidance for the Children of Israel.

And We made from among them leaders guiding by Our command when they were patient and had certainty in Our verses.

Indeed, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that over which they used to differ.

Has it not become clear to them how many generations We destroyed before them, in whose dwellings they now walk? Indeed in that are signs; will they not hear?

Have they not seen that We drive the water to a barren land and bring forth thereby crops that their livestock and they themselves eat? Will they not then see?

And they say, "When is this Judgement, if you are truthful?"

Say, "On the Day of Judgement the faith of those who disbelieved will not benefit them, nor will they be reprieved."

So turn away from them and wait; indeed, they are waiting.
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Jabir (RA): The Prophet (peace be upon him) would not sleep until he had recited Surah As-Sajdah and Surah Al-Mulk (Tabarak).

Jami at-Tirmidhi 3404

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Surah Al-Mulk
dua.dua_in_arabic:
بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِيْـمِ
(1) تَبَٰرَكَ ٱلَّذِى بِيَدِهِ ٱلۡمُلۡكُ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىۡءٍ قَدِيرٌ

(2) ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلۡمَوۡتَ وَٱلۡحَيَوٰةَ لِيَبۡلُوَكُمۡ أَيُّكُمۡ أَحۡسَنُ عَمَلاً‌ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلۡعَزِيزُ ٱلۡغَفُورُ

(3) ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ سَبۡعَ سَمَٰوَٰتٍ طِبَاقًا‌ۖ مَّا تَرَىٰ فِى خَلۡقِ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ مِن تَفَٰوُتٍ‌ۖ فَٱرۡجِعِ ٱلۡبَصَرَ هَلۡ تَرَىٰ مِن فُطُورٍ

(4) ثُمَّ ٱرۡجِعِ ٱلۡبَصَرَ كَرَّتَيۡنِ يَنقَلِبۡ إِلَيۡكَ ٱلۡبَصَرُ خَاسِئًا وَهُوَ حَسِيرٌ

(5) وَلَقَدۡ زَيَّنَّا ٱلسَّمَآءَ ٱلدُّنۡيَا بِمَصَٰبِيحَ وَجَعَلۡنَٰهَا رُجُومًا لِّلشَّيَٰطِينِ‌ۖ وَأَعۡتَدۡنَا لَهُمۡ عَذَابَ ٱلسَّعِيرِ

(6) وَلِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواۡ بِرَبِّهِمۡ عَذَابُ جَهَنَّمَ‌ۖ وَبِئۡسَ ٱلۡمَصِيرُ

(7) إِذَآ أُلۡقُواۡ فِيهَا سَمِعُواۡ لَهَا شَهِيقًا وَهِىَ تَفُورُ

(8) تَكَادُ تَمَيَّزُ مِنَ ٱلۡغَيۡظِ‌ۖ كُلَّمَآ أُلۡقِىَ فِيهَا فَوۡجٌ سَأَلَهُمۡ خَزَنَتُهَآ أَلَمۡ يَأۡتِكُمۡ نَذِيرٌ

(9) قَالُواۡ بَلَىٰ قَدۡ جَآءَنَا نَذِيرٌ فَكَذَّبۡنَا وَقُلۡنَا مَا نَزَّلَ ٱللَّهُ مِن شَىۡءٍ إِنۡ أَنتُمۡ إِلَّا فِى ضَلَٰلٍ كَبِيرٍ

(10) وَقَالُواۡ لَوۡ كُنَّا نَسۡمَعُ أَوۡ نَعۡقِلُ مَا كُنَّا فِىٓ أَصۡحَٰبِ ٱلسَّعِيرِ

(11) فَٱعۡتَرَفُواۡ بِذَنۢبِهِمۡ فَسُحۡقًا لِّأَصۡحَٰبِ ٱلسَّعِيرِ

(12) إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَخۡشَوۡنَ رَبَّهُم بِٱلۡغَيۡبِ لَهُم مَّغۡفِرَةٌ وَأَجۡرٌ كَبِيرٌ

(13) وَأَسِرُّواۡ قَوۡلَكُمۡ أَوِ ٱجۡهَرُواۡ بِهِۦٓ‌ۖ إِنَّهُۥ عَلِيمُۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ

(14) أَلَا يَعۡلَمُ مَنۡ خَلَقَ وَهُوَ ٱللَّطِيفُ ٱلۡخَبِيرُ

(15) هُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلۡأَرۡضَ ذَلُولاً فَٱمۡشُواۡ فِى مَنَاكِبِهَا وَكُلُواۡ مِن رِّزۡقِهِۦ‌ۖ وَإِلَيۡهِ ٱلنُّشُورُ

(16) ءَأَمِنتُم مَّن فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ أَن يَخۡسِفَ بِكُمُ ٱلۡأَرۡضَ فَإِذَا هِىَ تَمُورُ

(17) أَمۡ أَمِنتُم مَّن فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ أَن يُرۡسِلَ عَلَيۡكُمۡ حَاصِبًا‌ۖ فَسَتَعۡلَمُونَ كَيۡفَ نَذِيرِ

(18) وَلَقَدۡ كَذَّبَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبۡلِهِمۡ فَكَيۡفَ كَانَ نَكِيرِ

(19) أَوَلَمۡ يَرَوۡاۡ إِلَى ٱلطَّيۡرِ فَوۡقَهُمۡ صَـٰٓفَّٰتٍ وَيَقۡبِضۡنَ‌ۚ مَا يُمۡسِكُهُنَّ إِلَّا ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ‌ۚ إِنَّهُۥ بِكُلِّ شَىۡءِۭ بَصِيرٌ

(20) أَمَّنۡ هَٰذَا ٱلَّذِى هُوَ جُندٌ لَّكُمۡ يَنصُرُكُم مِّن دُونِ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ‌ۚ إِنِ ٱلۡكَٰفِرُونَ إِلَّا فِى غُرُورٍ

(21) أَمَّنۡ هَٰذَا ٱلَّذِى يَرۡزُقُكُمۡ إِنۡ أَمۡسَكَ رِزۡقَهُۥ‌ۚ بَل لَّجُّواۡ فِى عُتُوٍّ وَنُفُورٍ

(22) أَفَمَن يَمۡشِى مُكِبًّا عَلَىٰ وَجۡهِهِۦٓ أَهۡدَىٰٓ أَمَّن يَمۡشِى سَوِيًّا عَلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسۡتَقِيمٍ

(23) قُلۡ هُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنشَأَكُمۡ وَجَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلسَّمۡعَ وَٱلۡأَبۡصَٰرَ وَٱلۡأَفۡــِٔدَةَ‌ۖ قَلِيلاً مَّا تَشۡكُرُونَ

(24) قُلۡ هُوَ ٱلَّذِى ذَرَأَكُمۡ فِى ٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَإِلَيۡهِ تُحۡشَرُونَ

(25) وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَٰذَا ٱلۡوَعۡدُ إِن كُنتُمۡ صَٰدِقِينَ

(26) قُلۡ إِنَّمَا ٱلۡعِلۡمُ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ وَإِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ

(27) فَلَمَّا رَأَوۡهُ زُلۡفَةً سِيٓــَٔتۡ وُجُوهُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواۡ وَقِيلَ هَٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تَدَّعُونَ

(28) قُلۡ أَرَءَيۡتُمۡ إِنۡ أَهۡلَكَنِىَ ٱللَّهُ وَمَن مَّعِىَ أَوۡ رَحِمَنَا فَمَن يُجِيرُ ٱلۡكَٰفِرِينَ مِنۡ عَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ

(29) قُلۡ هُوَ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ ءَامَنَّا بِهِۦ وَعَلَيۡهِ تَوَكَّلۡنَا‌ۖ فَسَتَعۡلَمُونَ مَنۡ هُوَ فِى ضَلَٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ

(30) قُلۡ أَرَءَيۡتُمۡ إِنۡ أَصۡبَحَ مَآؤُكُمۡ غَوۡرًا فَمَن يَأۡتِيكُم بِمَآءٍ مَّعِينِۭ
dua.bangla_translation:
In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
Blessed is He in whose hand is dominion, and He is over all things competent -

Who created death and life to test you as to which of you is best in deed - and He is the Exalted in Might, the Forgiving -

Who created seven heavens in layers. You do not see in the creation of the Most Merciful any inconsistency. So return your vision [to the sky]; do you see any breaks?

Then return your vision twice again. Your vision will return to you humbled while it is fatigued.

And We have certainly beautified the nearest heaven with lamps and have made [from] them what is thrown at the devils, and We have prepared for them the punishment of the Blaze.

And for those who disbelieved in their Lord is the punishment of Hell, and wretched is the destination.

When they are thrown into it, they hear from it a dreadful inhaling while it boils up.

It almost bursts with rage. Every time a company is thrown into it, its keepers ask them, "Did there not come to you a warner?"

They will say, "Yes, a warner had come to us, but we denied and said, 'Allah has not sent down anything; you are only in great error.'"

And they will say, "If only we had been listening or reasoning, we would not be among the companions of the Blaze."

And they will admit their sin, so away with the companions of the Blaze.

Indeed, those who fear their Lord unseen will have forgiveness and a great reward.

And conceal your speech or make it known; indeed, He is Knowing of that within the breasts.

Does He who created not know, while He is the Subtle, the Acquainted?

It is He who made the earth tame for you, so walk among its slopes and eat of His provision, and to Him is the resurrection.

Do you feel secure that He who is above the heaven would not cause the earth to swallow you and suddenly it would sway?

Or do you feel secure that He who is above the heaven would not send against you a storm of stones? Then you would know how [severe] was My warning.

And already those before them had denied, and how [terrible] was My reproach.

Do they not see the birds above them with wings outspread and [sometimes] folded in? None holds them up except the Most Merciful. Indeed He is, of all things, Seeing.

Or who is it that could be an army for you to aid you other than the Most Merciful? The disbelievers are only in delusion.

Or who is it that could provide for you if He withheld His provision? But they have persisted in insolence and aversion.

Then is one who walks fallen on his face better guided, or one who walks upright on a straight path?

Say, "It is He who produced you and made for you hearing and vision and hearts; little are you grateful."

Say, "It is He who multiplied you throughout the earth, and to Him you will be gathered."

And they say, "When is this promise, if you are truthful?"

Say, "The knowledge is only with Allah, and I am only a clear warner."

But when they see it approaching, the faces of those who disbelieved will be distressed, and it will be said, "This is that for which you used to call."

Say, "Have you considered: whether Allah should cause my death and those with me, or have mercy upon us - who can protect the disbelievers from a painful punishment?"

Say, "He is the Most Merciful; we have believed in Him, and upon Him we rely. And you will [come to] know who it is that is in manifest error."

Say, "Have you considered: if your water were to sink into the earth, then who could bring you flowing water?"
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Jabir (RA): The Prophet (peace be upon him) would not sleep until he had recited Surah As-Sajdah and Surah Al-Mulk (Tabarak).

Jami at-Tirmidhi 3404

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Dua 1
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ بِاسْمِكَ اَمُوْتُ وَاَحْيَا
dua.dua_in_bangla: হে আল্লাহ! তোমার নামেই মৃত্যুলাভ (নিদ্রা) করছি এবং তোমার নামেই জীবিত (জাগ্রত) হব।
dua.bangla_translation: O Allah, in Your name I die and I live.
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Abu Dharr (RA): When the Prophet (peace be upon him) went to bed at night, he would say, "O Allah, in Your name I die and I live." And when he woke up he would say, "All praise is due to Allah who gave us life after having caused us to die, and to Him is the resurrection."

Sahih al-Bukhari 6325

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Dua 2
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ اَسْلَمْتُ وَجْهِيْ اِلَيْكَ، وَفَوَّضْتُ اَمْرِي اِلَيْكَ، وَاَلْجَاْتُ ظَهْرِيْ اِلَيْكَ، رَغْبَةً وَرَهْبَةً اِلَيْكَ، لَا مَلْجَاَ وَلاَ مَنْجَا مِنْكَ اِلَّا اِلَيْكَ، آمَنْتُ بِكِتَابِكَ الَّذِيْ اَنْزَلْتَ، وَبِنَبِيِّكَ الَّذِيْ اَرْسَلْتَ
dua.dua_in_bangla:
আল্লাহুম্মা আসলামতু ওয়াজহি ইলাইক,
ওয়া ফাওয়াওযতু আমরি ইলাইক,
ওয়া আলজা'তু জাহরি ইলাইক,
রাগবাতান ওয়া রাহবাতান ইলাইক,
লা মালজা'য়া ওয়া লা মানজা মিনকা ইল্লা ইলাইক,
আমানতু বিকিতাবিকা আল্লাজি আনযালত,
ওয়া বিনাবিয়িকা আল্লাজি আরসালত।
dua.bangla_translation: O Allah, I submit my face (my whole self) to You, I entrust my affairs to You, and I lay my back on You, out of fear and hope of You. There is no refuge nor safe haven from You except with You. I believe in Your Book which You revealed and in Your Prophet whom You sent.
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Al-Bara' ibn 'Azib (RA): The Prophet (peace be upon him) told him, "When you go to your bed, perform ablution as you do for prayer, then lie on your right side and say: O Allah, I submit my face to You, I entrust my affairs to You, and I lay my back on You, out of fear and hope of You. There is no refuge nor safe haven from You except with You. I believe in Your Book which You revealed and in Your Prophet whom You sent. If you die that night, you will die upon the natural faith (fitrah) of Islam, so let these words be the last thing you say." Al-Bara' said he tried to memorize it as "and in Your messenger whom You sent," but the Prophet corrected him to say "and in Your Prophet whom You sent."

Sahih al-Bukhari 6311

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Dua 3
dua.dua_in_arabic: بِاسْمِكَ رَبِّيْ وَضَعْتُ جَنْبِيْ، وَبِكَ اَرْفَعُهُ، اِنْ اَمْسَكْتَ نَفْسِيْ فَارْحَمْهَا، وَاِنْ اَرْسَلْتَهَا فَاحْفَظْهَا، بِمَا تَحْفَظُ بِهِ عِبَادَكَ الصَّالِحِيْنَ
dua.dua_in_bangla:
বিসমিকা রব্বি ওয়াদ'তু জানবি,
ওয়া বিকা আরফাউহু,
ইন আমসাকতা নাফসি ফারহামহা,
ওয়া ইন আরসালতাহা ফাহ্‌ফাযহা,
বিমা তাহ্‌ফাযু বিহি ইবাদাকাস সালিহীন।
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, in Your name I lay down my body, and in Your name I will raise it up. If You should take my soul, have mercy upon it, and if You should return it, protect it as You protect Your righteous servants.
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Abu Hurairah (RA): The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "When one of you goes to his bed, let him dust it off with the inside of his lower garment, for he does not know what came onto it after he left it. Then let him say: My Lord, in Your name I lay down my body, and in Your name I will raise it up. If You should take my soul, have mercy upon it, and if You should return it, protect it as You protect Your righteous servants."

Sahih al-Bukhari 6320

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Dua 4
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ قِنِيْ عَذَابَكَ يَوْمَ تَبْعَثُ عِبَادَكَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আল্লাহুম্মা কিন্নী আযাবাকা ইয়াওমা তাব’আসু ইবাদাকা ।
dua.bangla_translation: O Allah, protect me from Your punishment on the Day You resurrect Your servants.
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated from Hafsah (RA), wife of the Prophet (peace be upon him): When the Prophet (peace be upon him) lay down, he would place his right hand under his cheek and say three times: "O Allah, protect me from Your punishment on the Day You resurrect Your servants."

Sunan Abu Dawud 5045

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Dua 5
dua.dua_in_arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ الَّذِي اَطْعَمَنَا وَسَقَانَا، وَكَفَانَا وَاٰوَانَا، فَكَمْ مِمَّنْ لاَ كَافِيَ لَهُ وَلاَ مُئْوِيَ
dua.dua_in_bangla:
আলহামদু লিল্লা-হিল্লাযী আত- আমানা ওয়া সাকা-না
ওয়া কাফা-না- ওয়া আ-ওয়া-না
ফাকাম মিম্মান লা-কা-ফিয়া লাহু ওয়ালা- মু’বিয় ।
dua.bangla_translation: All praise is due to Allah, who has fed us and given us drink, and has been sufficient for us and given us shelter. How many are there who have no one to suffice them or give them shelter.
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Anas (RA): When the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) went to his bed, he would say, "All praise is due to Allah, who has fed us and given us drink, and has been sufficient for us and given us shelter. How many are there who have no one to suffice them or give them shelter."

Sahih Muslim 2715

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Dua 6
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ اِنَّكَ خَلَقْتَ نَفْسِيْ، وَاَنْتَ تَوَفَّاهَا، لَكَ مَمَاتُهَا وَمَحْيَاهَا، اِنْ اَحْيَيْتَهَا فَاحْفَظْهَا، وَاِنْ اَمَتَّهَا فَاغْفِرْ لَهَا، اللَّهُمَّ اِنِّيْ اَسْاَلُكَ الْعَافِيَةَ
dua.dua_in_bangla:
আল্লাহুম্মা ইন্নাকা খলাকতা নাফসী,
ওয়া আনতা তাওয়াফ্‌ফা'হা,
লাকা মামা'তুহা ওয়া মাহইয়া'হা,
ইন্‌ আহ ইয়াইতাহা ফাহফাযহা,
ওয়া ইন্‌ আমাত্‌তাহা ফাগফির লাহা,
আল্লহুম্মা ইন্নী আস্আলুকাল আফিয়াহ ।
dua.bangla_translation: O Allah, You created my soul and You take it back. To You belongs its death and its life. If You keep it alive, then protect it, and if You cause it to die, then forgive it. O Allah, I ask You for well-being.
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Abdullah ibn Umar (RA): He instructed a man that when he goes to bed he should say, "O Allah, You created my soul and You take it back. To You belongs its death and its life. If You keep it alive, then protect it, and if You cause it to die, then forgive it. O Allah, I ask You for well-being." The man asked, "Did you hear this from Umar?" He replied, "From one better than Umar - the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him)."

Sahih Muslim 2712

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Dua 7
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَرَبَّ الْاَرْضِ وَرَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيْمِ، رَبَّنَا وَرَبَّ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ، فَالِقَ الْحَبِّ وَالنَّوٰى وَمُنْزِلَ التَّوْرَاةِ وَالاِنْجِيْلِ وَالْفُرْقَانِ، اَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ اَنْتَ اٰخِذٌ بِنَاصِيَتِهِ، اَللَّهُـمَّ اَنْتَ الْاَوَّلُ فَلَيْسَ قَبْلَكَ شَىْءٌ، وَاَنْتَ الاٰخِرُ فَلَيْسَ بَعْدَكَ شَىْءٌ، وَاَنْتَ الظَّاهِرُ فَلَيْسَ فَوْقَكَ شَىْءٌ، وَاَنْتَ الْبَاطِنُ فَلَيْسَ دُوْنَكَ شَىْءٌ، اقْضِ عَنَّا الدَّيْنَ وَاَغْنِنَا مِنَ الْفَقْرِ
dua.dua_in_bangla:
আল্ল-হুম্মা রব্বাস সামা-ওয়া-তি ওয়া রব্বাল আরযি ওয়া রব্বাল আরশিল আযীম,
রব্বানা ওয়া রব্বা কুল্লি শাইয়িন্‌
ফা-লিকাল হাব্বি ওয়ান্‌ নাওয়া ওয়া মুনযিলাত তাওরা-তি ওয়াল ইনজীলি ওয়াল ফুরকান,
আউযুবিকা মিন শাররি কুল্লি শাইয়িন আনতা আ-খিযুন বিনা-সিয়াতিহি,
আল্ল-হুম্মা আন্‌তাল আওওয়ালু ফালাইসা কাবলাকা শাইউন ওয়া আনতাল আ-খিরু ফালাইসা বা’দাকা শাইউন,
ওয়া আনতায যা-হিরু ফালাইসা ফাওকাকা শাইউন ওয়া আনতাল বা-তিনু ফালাইসা দূনাকা শাইউন,
ইকযি আন্নাদ দাইনা ওয়া আগনিনা- মিনাল ফাক্‌রি।
dua.bangla_translation: O Allah, Lord of the heavens and Lord of the earth and Lord of the Mighty Throne, our Lord and the Lord of all things, Splitter of the grain and the date-stone, Revealer of the Torah, the Injeel, and the Furqan (Quran), I seek refuge in You from the evil of everything whose forelock You hold. O Allah, You are the First, so there is nothing before You, and You are the Last, so there is nothing after You. You are the Manifest, so there is nothing above You, and You are the Hidden, so there is nothing beyond You. Settle our debts for us and spare us from poverty.
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Suhail (RA): Abu Salih used to instruct them that when one of them goes to bed, he should lie on his right side and say the above dua. He would relate this hadith from the Prophet (peace be upon him) through Abu Hurairah (RA).

Sahih Muslim 2713

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Dua 8
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَللَّهُـمَّ اِنِّيْ اَعُوْذُ بِوَجْهِكَ الْكَرِيْمِ، وَكَلِمَاتِكَ التَّامَّةِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا اَنْتَ اٰخِذٌ بِنَاصِيَتِهِ، اَللَّهُمَّ اَنْتَ تَكْشِفُ الْمَغْرَمَ وَالْمَاْثَمَ، اَللَّهُمَّ لاَ يُهْزَمُ جُنْدُكَ وَلاَ يُخْلَفُ وَعْدُكَ وَلاَ يَنْفَعُ ذَا الْجَدِّ مِنْكَ الْجَدُّ، سُبْحَانَكَ وَبِحَمْدِكَ ‏
dua.dua_in_bangla:
আললাহুমমা ইন্নি আউযুবিকা বি ওয়াজহিকাল কারীম,
ওয়া কালিমাতিকাত তাম্মাতি মিন শাররি মা আনতা আখিযুম বি নাসিয়াতিহি, 
আললাহুমমা আনতা তাকশিফুল মাগরামা ওয়াল মা’সাম,
আললাহুমমা লা ইউহযামু জুনদুকা,ওয়া লা ইয়ুখলাফু,
ওয়াদুকা ওয়া লা ইয়ানফাউ যাল জাদ্দি মিনকাল জাদ্দু,
সুবহানাকা ওয়া বিহামদিকা।
dua.bangla_translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in Your perfect words from the evil of what You have created. O Allah, You are the One who removes the burden of debt and sin. O Allah, Your army cannot be defeated, Your promise cannot be broken, and the wealth of the wealthy cannot save him from You. Glory be to You, and praise be to You.
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Ali (RA): The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him), when lying down on his bed, would say the above dua.

Sunan Abu Dawud 5052

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Dua 9
dua.dua_in_arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ وَضَعْتُ جَنْبِيْ، اَللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِيْ ذَنْبِيْ وَاَخْسِئْ شَيْطَانِي، وَفُكَّ رِهَانِيْ وَاجْعَلْنِيْ فِي النَّدِيِّ الْاَعْلٰى
dua.dua_in_bangla:
বিসমিল্লাহি ওয়াদ'আতু জান্বি
আল্লাহুম্মাগফিরলি জুনুবি ওয়া আখসি শাইতানী,
ওয়া ফুক্কা রিহানী ওয়া ইজ'আলনী ফিন্নাদিয়্যিল আলা ।
dua.bangla_translation: In the name of Allah I lay down my body. O Allah, forgive my sins, drive Satan away from me, free my neck [from the Fire], and place me among those of high rank.
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Abul Azhar Al-Anmari (RA): The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him), when lying down at night, would say the above dua.

Sunan Abu Dawud 5054

dua.title: Before Sleeping - Dua 10
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ الَّذِيْ كَفَانِيْ وَآوَانِيْ، وَاَطْعَمَنِيْ وَسَقَانِيْ وَالَّذِيْ مَنَّ عَلَىَّ فَاَفْضَلَ، وَالَّذِيْ اَعْطَانِيْ فَاَجْزَلَ الْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ عَلٰى كُلِّ حَالٍ، اَللَّهُـمَّ رَبَّ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ وَمَلِيْكَهُ، وَاِلٰهَ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ اَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنَ النَّارِ
dua.dua_in_bangla:
আলহামদুলিল্লাহিল্লাজী কাফানী ওয়া আওয়ানী
ওয়া আত'আমানী ওয়া সাক্বানী ওয়াল্লাজী মান্না আলাইয়া ফা আফদ্বালা,
ওয়াল্লাজী আত'আনী ফাজ'জালা আলহামদুলিল্লাহি আলা কুল্লি হালিন,
আল্লাহুম্মা রব্বা কুল্লি শাইয়িঁ ওয়া মালিকাহ,
ওয়া ইলাহা কুল্লি শাইয়িঁ আউযুবিকা মিনান্নার।
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Ibn Umar (RA): When the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) went to bed at night, he would say, "All praise and thanks are due to Allah, who has been immensely kind to me and has bestowed upon me. So praise and thanks to Allah in every state. O Allah, Lord and Owner of everything, and God of everything, I seek refuge in You from the punishment of the Fire."

Sunan Abu Dawud 5058

dua.title: Upon Waking - Dua 1
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ الَّذِيْ اَحْيَانَا بَعْدَ مَا اَمَاتَنَا وَ اِلَيْهِ النُّشُوْرُ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আলহামদু লিল্লাা হিল্লাযী আহ ইয়াানাা বা‘দা মাা আমাা তানাা ওয়া ইলাইহিন নুশূর।
dua.bangla_translation: All praise is due to Allah, who gave us life after having caused us to die, and to Him is the resurrection.

dua.title: Upon Waking - Dua
dua.dua_in_arabic: لَآ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللّٰهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيْكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيْرٌ، سُبْحَانَ اللّٰهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ، وَلَآ إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللّٰهُ، وَاللّٰهُ أَكْبَرُ، وَلَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللّٰهِ الْعَلِيِّ الْعَظِيْمِ، رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِيٍْ
dua.dua_in_bangla: লা ইলা-হা ইল্লাল্লা-হু ওয়াহ্‌দাহূ লা- শারীকালাহূ, লাহুল মুলকু, ওয়ালাহুল হামদু, ওয়াহুয়া ‘আলা কুল্লি শায়ইন ক্বাদীর। সুবহা-নাল্লাহি, ওয়ালহামদু লিল্লাহি, ওয়া লা ইলা-হা ইল্লাল্লা-হু, ওয়াল্লা-হু আকবার, ওয়া লা- হাওলা ওয়ালা- কুওয়াতা ইল্লা- বিল্লা-হিল ‘আলিয়্যিল ‘আযীম, রাব্বিগফির লী
dua.bangla_translation: There is no true god except Allah alone, who has no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is over all things competent. Glory be to Allah. All praise is due to Allah. There is no true god except Allah. Allah is the Greatest. There is no might and no power except by Allah, the Most High, the Most Great. My Lord, forgive me.

dua.title: Upon Waking - Dua
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ الَّذِيْ عَافَانِيْ فِيْ جَسَدِي، وَرَدَّ عَلَيَّ رُوْحِي، وَأَذِنَ لِيْ بِذِكْرِهِ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আল্‌হামদু লিল্লা-হিল্লাযী ‘আ-ফা-নী ফী জাসাদী, ওয়ারদ্দা ‘আলাইয়্যা রূহী ওয়া আযিনা লী বিযিকরিহী
dua.bangla_translation: All praise is due to Allah, who kept my body safe, returned my soul to me, and permitted me to remember Him.

dua.title: Protection from the Trial of Dajjal - Amol 1
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَلۡحَمۡدُ لِلّٰهِ الَّذِیۡۤ اَنۡزَلَ عَلٰی عَبۡدِهِ الۡکِتٰبَ وَ لَمۡ یَجۡعَلۡ لَّهٗ عِوَجًا ؕ﴿ٜ۱﴾ قَیِّمًا لِّیُنۡذِرَ بَاۡسًا شَدِیۡدًا مِّنۡ لَّدُنۡهُ وَ یُبَشِّرَ الۡمُؤۡمِنِیۡنَ الَّذِیۡنَ یَعۡمَلُوۡنَ الصّٰلِحٰتِ اَنَّ لَهُمۡ اَجۡرًا حَسَنًا ۙ﴿۲﴾ مَّاکِثِیۡنَ فِیۡهِ اَبَدًا ۙ﴿۳﴾ وَّ یُنۡذِرَ الَّذِیۡنَ قَالُوا اتَّخَذَ اللّٰهُ وَلَدًا ٭﴿۴﴾ مَا لَهُمۡ بِهٖ مِنۡ عِلۡمٍ وَّ لَا لِاٰبَآئِهِمۡ ؕ کَبُرَتۡ کَلِمَۃً تَخۡرُجُ مِنۡ اَفۡوَاهِهِمۡ ؕ اِنۡ یَّقُوۡلُوۡنَ اِلَّا کَذِبًا ﴿۵﴾ فَلَعَلَّکَ بَاخِعٌ نَّفۡسَکَ عَلٰۤی اٰثَارِهِمۡ اِنۡ لَّمۡ یُؤۡمِنُوۡا بِهٰذَا الۡحَدِیۡثِ اَسَفًا ﴿۶﴾ اِنَّا جَعَلۡنَا مَا عَلَی الۡاَرۡضِ زِیۡنَۃً لَّهَا لِنَبۡلُوَهُمۡ اَیُّهُمۡ اَحۡسَنُ عَمَلًا ﴿۷﴾ وَ اِنَّا لَجٰعِلُوۡنَ مَا عَلَیۡهَا صَعِیۡدًا جُرُزًا ؕ﴿۸﴾ اَمۡ حَسِبۡتَ اَنَّ اَصۡحٰبَ الۡکَهۡفِ وَ الرَّقِیۡمِ ۙ کَانُوۡا مِنۡ اٰیٰتِنَا عَجَبًا ﴿۹﴾ اِذۡ اَوَی الۡفِتۡیَۃُ اِلَی الۡکَهۡفِ فَقَالُوۡا رَبَّنَاۤ اٰتِنَا مِنۡ لَّدُنۡکَ رَحۡمَۃً وَّ هَیِّیٴۡ لَنَا مِنۡ اَمۡرِنَا رَشَدًا ﴿۱۰﴾
dua.bangla_translation: All praise is due to Allah, who has sent down upon His Servant the Book and has not made therein any deviance. [He has made it] straight, to warn of severe punishment from Him and to give good tidings to the believers who do righteous deeds that they will have a good reward, in which they will remain forever. And to warn those who say, "Allah has taken a son." They have no knowledge of it, nor had their fathers. Grave is the word that comes out of their mouths; they speak not except a lie. Then perhaps you would kill yourself through grief over them, if they do not believe in this message, out of sorrow. Indeed, We have made that which is on the earth an adornment for it, that We may test them [as to] which of them is best in deed. And indeed, We will make what is upon it a barren ground. Or have you thought that the companions of the cave and the inscription were, among Our signs, a wonder? [Mention] when the youths retreated to the cave and said, "Our Lord, grant us from Yourself mercy and prepare for us from our affair right guidance."
dua.rules: Recite the first 10 verses of Surah Al-Kahf every Friday

dua.title: Protection from the Trial of Dajjal - Amol 2
dua.dua_in_arabic: الَّذِيْنَ كَانَتْ اَعْيُنُهُمْ فِيْ غِطَـآءٍ عَنْ ذِكْرِيْ وَكَانُوْا لَا يَسْتَطِيْعُوْنَ سَمْعًا  (101) اَفَحَسِبَ الَّذِيْنَ كَفَرُوْۤا اَنْ يَّتَّخِذُوْا عِبَادِيْ مِنْ دُوْنِيْۤ اَوْلِيَآءَ ؕ اِنَّـاۤ اَعْتَدْنَا جَهَنَّمَ لِلْكٰفِرِيْنَ نُزُلًا (102) قُلْ هَلْ نُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِالْاَخْسَرِيْنَ اَعْمَالًا ؕ  (103) اَلَّذِيْنَ ضَلَّ سَعْيُهُمْ فِي الْحَيٰوةِ الدُّنْيَا وَهُمْ يَحْسَبُوْنَ اَنَّهُمْ يُحْسِنُوْنَ صُنْعًا (104) اُولٰٓئِكَ الَّذِيْنَ كَفَرُوْا بِاٰيٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ وَلِقَآئِهٖ فَحَبِطَتْ اَعْمَالُهُمْ فَلَا نُقِيْمُ لَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيٰمَةِ وَزْنًا (105) ذٰلِكَ جَزَآؤُهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ بِمَا كَفَرُوْا وَاتَّخَذُوْۤا اٰيٰتِيْ وَرُسُلِيْ هُزُوًا (106) اِنَّ الَّذِيْنَ اٰمَنُوْا وَعَمِلُوا الصّٰلِحٰتِ كَانَتْ لَهُمْ جَنّٰتُ الْفِرْدَوْسِ نُزُلًا ۙ  (107) خٰلِدِيْنَ فِيْهَا لَا يَبْغُوْنَ عَنْهَا حِوَلًا (108) قُلْ لَّوْ كَانَ الْبَحْرُ مِدَادًا لِّكَلِمٰتِ رَبِّيْ لَنَفِدَ الْبَحْرُ قَبْلَ اَنْ تَنْفَدَ كَلِمٰتُ رَبِّيْ وَلَوْ جِئْنَا بِمِثْلِهٖ مَدَدًا (109) قُلْ اِنَّمَاۤ اَنَا بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُكُمْ يُوْحٰۤي اِلَيَّ اَنَّمَاۤ اِلٰـهُكُمْ اِلٰهٌ وَّاحِدٌ ۚ  فَمَنْ كَانَ يَرْجُوْا لِقَآءَ رَبِّهٖ فَلْيَعْمَلْ عَمَلًا صَالِحًا وَّلَا يُشْرِكْ بِعِبَادَةِ رَبِّهٖۤ اَحَدًا  (110)
dua.bangla_translation: [They were] those whose eyes had been within a cover [removed] from My remembrance, and they were not able to hear. Then do those who disbelieve think that they can take My servants as allies instead of Me? Indeed, We have prepared Hell for the disbelievers as a lodging. Say, "Shall we inform you of the greatest losers as to [their] deeds? [They are] those whose effort is lost in worldly life, while they think that they are doing well." Those are the ones who disbelieve in the verses of their Lord and in [their] meeting Him, so their deeds have become worthless, and We will not assign them any weight on the Day of Resurrection. That is their recompense - Hell - for what they denied and [because] they took My signs and My messengers in ridicule. Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds will have the Gardens of Paradise as a lodging, wherein they abide eternally, never desiring to leave. Say, "If the sea were ink for [writing] the words of my Lord, the sea would be exhausted before the words of my Lord were exhausted, even if We brought the like of it as a supplement." Say, "I am only a man like you, to whom it has been revealed that your god is one God. So whoever hopes for the meeting with his Lord, let him do righteous work and associate none in the worship of his Lord."
dua.rules: The last 10 verses of Surah Al-Kahf are also mentioned in a hadith

dua.title: Dua for Steadfastness on Guidance
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تُزِغْ قُلُوبَنَا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنَا وَهَبْ لَنَا مِنْ لَدُنْكَ رَحْمَةً إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْوَهَّابُ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বানা লা তুযিগ ক্বুলূবানা বা'দা ইয হাদাইতানা ওয়া হাব লানা মিন লাদুনকা রহমাতান ইন্নাকা আনতাল ওয়াহহাব
dua.bangla_translation: Our Lord, let not our hearts deviate after You have guided us, and grant us from Yourself mercy. Indeed, You are the Bestower.

dua.title: Dua for Having a Child
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ اِنِّیۡ وَهَنَ الۡعَظۡمُ مِنِّیۡ وَ اشۡتَعَلَ الرَّاۡسُ شَیۡبًا وَّ لَمۡ اَکُنۡۢ بِدُعَآئِکَ رَبِّ شَقِیًّا. وَ اِنِّیۡ خِفۡتُ الۡمَوَالِیَ مِنۡ وَّرَآءِیۡ وَ کَانَتِ امۡرَاَتِیۡ عَاقِرًا فَهَبۡ لِیۡ مِنۡ لَّدُنۡکَ وَلِیًّا
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বি ইন্নি ওয়া হানাল আজমু মিন্নী ওয়াশতাআলার রসু শাইবাও ওয়ালাম আকুম বিদুআয়িকা রব্বি শাকিয়্যা। ওয়া ইন্নি খিফতুল মাওয়ালিয়া মিয়্যাওরয়ি ওয়া কানাতিম রআতী আকিরান ফাহাবলি মিল্লাদুনকা ওলিয়্যা।
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, indeed my bones have weakened, and my head has filled with white hair, and never have I been in my supplication to You, my Lord, unhappy. And indeed, I fear the successors after me, and my wife has been barren, so give me from Yourself an heir.
dua.footnote: Prophet Zakariya's (AS) prayer to Allah for a righteous child, despite his wife's barrenness

dua.title: Dua for Having a Child - 2
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ لَا تَذَرْنِي فَرْدًا وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الْوَارِثِينَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বি লা তাযারনী ফারদাও ওয়া আনতা খাইরুল ওয়ারিছিন
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, do not leave me alone [with no heir], while You are the best of inheritors.
dua.footnote: Prophet Zakariya's (AS) prayer, desiring a child

dua.title: Dua for Refuge from Satan's Incitement
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ هَمَزَاتِ الشَّيَاطِينِ. وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ رَبِّ أَنْ يَحْضُرُونِ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বি আউযু বিকা মিন হামাযাতিশ শায়াত্বীন, ওয়া আউযু বিকা রব্বি আইয়াহ দুরুন।
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, I seek refuge in You from the incitements of the devils, and I seek refuge in You, my Lord, lest they be present with me.

dua.title: Dua to Be Among the Witnesses of Truth
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبَّنَا آمَنَّا بِمَا أَنْزَلْتَ وَاتَّبَعْنَا الرَّسُولَ فَاكْتُبْنَا مَعَ الشَّاهِدِينَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বানা আমান্না বিমা আনযালতা ওয়াত্তাবানার রাসূলা ফাকতুবনা মাআশ শাহিদীন।
dua.bangla_translation: Our Lord, we have believed in what You revealed and followed the messenger, so register us among the witnesses [to truth].

dua.title: Prayer for Gratitude and to Be Among the Righteous
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ اَوۡزِعۡنِیۡۤ اَنۡ اَشۡکُرَ نِعۡمَتَکَ الَّتِیۡۤ اَنۡعَمۡتَ عَلَیَّ وَ عَلٰی وَالِدَیَّ وَ اَنۡ اَعۡمَلَ صَالِحًا تَرۡضٰىهُ وَ اَدۡخِلۡنِیۡ بِرَحۡمَتِکَ فِیۡ عِبَادِکَ الصّٰلِحِیۡنَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বি আওযি’নী আন আশকুরা নিআমাতাকাল লাতী আনআমতা আলাইয়্যা ওয়া আলা ওয়ালি দাইয়্যা ওয়াআন আ”মালা ছলিহান তারদ্বয়াহু ওয়া আদখিলনী বিরহমাতিকা ফি ইবাদিকাছ ছলিহীন
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favor which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents, and to do righteousness of which You approve. And admit me, by Your mercy, into [the ranks of] Your righteous servants.
dua.footnote: Prophet Sulaiman's (AS) prayer of gratitude to Allah, after learning by Allah's grace how an ant warned others to avoid being crushed unknowingly by his army

dua.title: Dua for Sustenance
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبَّنَاۤ اَنۡزِلۡ عَلَیۡنَا مَآئِدَۃً مِّنَ السَّمَآءِ تَکُوۡنُ لَنَا عِیۡدًا لِّاَوَّلِنَا وَ اٰخِرِنَا وَ اٰیَۃً مِّنۡکَ ۚ وَ ارۡزُقۡنَا وَ اَنۡتَ خَیۡرُ الرّٰزِقِیۡنَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বানা আনযিল আলাইনা মায়িদাতাম মিনাস সামায়ি তাকূনু লানা ঈদাল আওয়্যালিনা ও আখিরনা ও আয়াতাম মিনকা, ওয়ারযুকনা ওয়া আনতা খাইরুর রাযিক্বীন।
dua.bangla_translation: O Allah, our Lord, send down to us a table [spread with food] from the heaven to be for us a festival for the first of us and the last of us, and a sign from You. And provide for us, and You are the best of providers.
dua.footnote: Prophet Isa's (AS) dua to Allah

dua.title: Prayer for Mercy and Right Guidance (Dua of the People of the Cave)
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا مِنْ لَدُنْكَ رَحْمَةً وَهَيِّئْ لَنَا مِنْ أَمْرِنَا رَشَدًا
dua.dua_in_bangla: হে আমাদের প্রভূ! আপনি আপনার পক্ষ থেকে আমাদের কে রহমত দান করুন এবং আপনি আমাদের জন্য আমাদের কাজের একটি সঠিক পথ নির্ধারণ করে দিন।
dua.bangla_translation: Our Lord, grant us from Yourself mercy and prepare for us from our affair right guidance.
dua.footnote: The believing youths of the Companions of the Cave, fleeing severe persecution from a polytheist nation, took refuge in a mountain cave and made this prayer to Allah

dua.title: Dua for Help in State Affairs
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ أَدْخِلْنِي مُدْخَلَ صِدْقٍ وَأَخْرِجْنِي مُخْرَجَ صِدْقٍ وَاجْعَلْ لِي مِنْ لَدُنْكَ سُلْطَانًا نَصِيرًا
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বি আদখিলনী মুদখলা ছিদকিন ওয়া আখরিজনী মুখরজা ছিদকিনও ওয়াজআলনী মিললাদুনকা সুলতানান নাছীরা
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, cause me to enter a sound entrance and to exit a sound exit and grant me from Yourself a supporting authority.
dua.footnote: The Prophet (peace be upon him) made this dua to Allah before gaining state authority in Madinah, for the establishment of a just society and government

dua.title: Dua When Boarding a Vehicle
dua.dua_in_arabic: سُبۡحٰنَ الَّذِیۡ سَخَّرَ لَنَا هٰذَا وَ مَا کُنَّا لَهٗ مُقۡرِنِیۡنَ. وَ اِنَّاۤ اِلٰی رَبِّنَا لَمُنۡقَلِبُوۡنَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: সুবহানাল্লাযি শাখখারলানা হাজা ওমা কুন্না লাহু মুক্বরিনীনা , ওয়া ইন্না ইলা রব্বিনা লা মুনক্বলিবুন
dua.bangla_translation: Exalted is He who has subjected this to us, and we could not have [otherwise] subdued it. And indeed we, to our Lord, will surely return.

dua.title: Dua to Die as a Muslim and Join the Righteous
dua.dua_in_arabic: فَاطِرَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ أَنْتَ وَلِيِّي فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ تَوَفَّنِي مُسْلِمًا وَأَلْحِقْنِي بِالصَّالِحِينَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: ফাত্বিরাছ ছামাওয়াতি ওয়াল আরদি আনতা ওয়ালিইয়্যী ফিদ দুনইয়া ওয়াল আখিরাহ, তাওয়াফফানী মুসলিমাও ওয়া আলহিকনী বিছছালিহীন
dua.bangla_translation: Creator of the heavens and earth, You are my protector in this world and in the Hereafter. Cause me to die as a Muslim and join me with the righteous.
dua.footnote: Prophet Yusuf's (AS) prayer of gratitude to Allah after being granted authority and honored throughout his life

dua.title: Dua for Deliverance from Hardship
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ لَوۡ شِئۡتَ اَهۡلَکۡتَهُمۡ مِّنۡ قَبۡلُ وَ اِیَّایَ ؕ اَتُهۡلِکُنَا بِمَا فَعَلَ السُّفَهَآءُ مِنَّا ۚ اِنۡ هِیَ اِلَّا فِتۡنَتُکَ ؕ تُضِلُّ بِهَا مَنۡ تَشَآءُ وَ تَهۡدِیۡ مَنۡ تَشَآءُ ؕ اَنۡتَ وَلِیُّنَا فَاغۡفِرۡ لَنَا وَ ارۡحَمۡنَا وَ اَنۡتَ خَیۡرُ الۡغٰفِرِیۡنَ. وَ اکۡتُبۡ لَنَا فِیۡ هٰذِهِ الدُّنۡیَا حَسَنَۃً وَّ فِی الۡاٰخِرَۃِ اِنَّا هُدۡنَاۤ اِلَیۡکَ ؕ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বি লাও শি’তা আহলাকতাহুম মিন ক্ববলু ওয়া ইয়্যা ইয়া আতুহলিকুনা বিমা ফাআলা স সুফাহা-য়ু মিন্না ইন হিয়া ইল্লা ফিতনাতুকা তুদ্বিলু বিহা মান তাশা-য়ু ওয়তাহদী মান তাশাউ আনতা ওয়ালিয়্যুনা ফাগফির লানা ওয়ারহামনা ওয়া আনতা খাইরুরল গফিরীন।ওয়াকতুব লানা ফি হাজিহীদ দুনইয়া হাসানাতাও ওয়াফিল আখিরাতি ইন্না হুদনা ইলাইক।
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, if You had willed, You could have destroyed them before, and me [as well]. Would You destroy us for what the foolish among us have done? This is not but Your trial, by which You send astray whom You will and guide whom You will. You are our Protector, so forgive us and have mercy upon us, and You are the best of forgivers. And decree for us in this world [that which is] good, and [also] in the Hereafter; indeed, we have turned back to You.
dua.footnote: Prophet Musa (AS) recited this dua when he and the seventy men chosen from his people were struck by an earthquake while proceeding to the place appointed by Allah

dua.title: Dua for Deliverance from Calamity
dua.dua_in_arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنْتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: লা ইলাহা ইল্লা আনতা সুবহানকা ইন্নী কুনতু মিনায যালিমীন
dua.bangla_translation: There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers.
dua.footnote: Prophet Yunus's (AS) prayer to Allah while in darkness inside the whale's belly

dua.title: Dua for Parents
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ ارْحَمْهُمَا كَمَا رَبَّيَانِي صَغِيرًا
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বির হামহুমা কামা রব্বায়ানী ছগীরা
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, have mercy upon them as they brought me up when I was small.

dua.title: Prayer for Forgiveness of Sin
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَا أَنْفُسَنَا وَإِنْ لَمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنَا وَتَرْحَمْنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বানা যলামনা আনফুছানা ওয়া ইল্লামতাগফির লানা ওয়া তারহামনা লানাকূনান্না মিনাল খাছিরীন
dua.bangla_translation: Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves, and if You do not forgive us and have mercy upon us, we will surely be among the losers.
dua.footnote: The repentant prayer of Prophet Adam (AS) and Mother Hawwa (AS) after eating from the forbidden tree at Satan's instigation

dua.title: Prayer for Wisdom and a Lasting Good Reputation
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ هَبۡ لِیۡ حُکۡمًا وَّ اَلۡحِقۡنِیۡ بِالصّٰلِحِیۡنَ. وَ اجۡعَلۡ لِّیۡ لِسَانَ صِدۡقٍ فِی الۡاٰخِرِیۡنَ. وَ اجۡعَلۡنِیۡ مِنۡ وَّرَثَۃِ جَنَّۃِ النَّعِیۡمِ. وَلَا تُخْزِنِي يَوْمَ يُبْعَثُونَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বি হাবলি হুকমাও ওআলহিকনী বিছছলিহীন। অজ আল্লী লিসানা ছিদকীন ফিল আখিরীন। অজ আলনী মিয়্যও অরছাতি জান্নাতিন নাঈম। ওয়া লা তুখযিনী ইয়াওমা ইউবআছুন।
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, grant me judgement [i.e. wisdom and knowledge] and join me with the righteous. And grant me a reputation of honor among later generations. And make me among the inheritors of the Garden of Bliss. And do not disgrace me on the Day they are resurrected.
dua.footnote: Prophet Ibrahim's (AS) dua

dua.title: Prayer for Gratitude for Blessings, Opportunity for Good Deeds, and Righteous Offspring
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِي أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ وَعَلَى وَالِدَيَّ وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صَالِحًا تَرْضَاهُ وَأَصْلِحْ لِي فِي ذُرِّيَّتِي إِنِّي تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ وَإِنِّي مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বি আওযি'নী আন আশকুরা নি'মাতাকাল্লাতী আনআমতা আলাইয়্যা ওয়া আলা ওয়ালিদাইয়্যা ওয়া আন আমালা ছালিহান তারদাহু ওয়া আছলিহ লী ফী যুররিয়্যাতী ইন্নী তুবতু ইলাইকা ওয়া ইন্নী মিনাল মুসলিমীন
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favor which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents and to do righteousness of which You will approve, and make righteous for me my offspring. Indeed, I have repented to You, and indeed, I am of the Muslims.

dua.title: Prayer for Forgiveness for Parents and Believers
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لِي وَلِوَالِدَيَّ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يَوْمَ يَقُومُ الْحِسَابُ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বানাগ ফির লী ওয়ালি ওয়ালিদাইয়্যা ওয়া লিলমুমিনীনা ইয়াওমা ইয়াকূমুল হিসাব
dua.bangla_translation: Our Lord, forgive me and my parents and the believers the Day the account is established.

dua.title: Prayer to Be Steadfast in Prayer, and for One's Offspring
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ اجْعَلْنِي مُقِيمَ الصَّلَاةِ وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي رَبَّنَا وَتَقَبَّلْ دُعَاءِ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বিজ্আলনী মুকীমাছ ছলাতি ওয়া মিন যুররিইয়্যাতী রব্বানা ওয়া তাকব্বাল দোয়া
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, make me an establisher of prayer, and [many] from my descendants. Our Lord, and accept my supplication.
dua.footnote: Prophet Ibrahim's (AS) dua to Allah that he and his descendants be accepted as establishers of prayer

dua.title: Dua for a Righteous Child
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ هَبْ لِي مِنْ لَدُنْكَ ذُرِّيَّةً طَيِّبَةً إِنَّكَ سَمِيعُ الدُّعَاءِ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বি হাব লী মিল লাদুনকা যুররিয়্যাতান ত্বইয়্যিবা, ইন্নাকা ছামীউদ দোয়া
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, grant me from Yourself a good offspring. Indeed, You are the Hearer of supplication.
dua.footnote: Prophet Zakariya's (AS) dua to Allah for a righteous child

dua.title: Dua for a Righteous Child - 2
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ هَبْ لِي مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বি হাব লী মিনাছছালিহীন
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, grant me [a child] from among the righteous.
dua.footnote: Prophet Ibrahim's (AS) dua to Allah for a righteous child

dua.title: Dua for Refuge from Women's Temptation
dua.dua_in_arabic: مَعَاذَ اللّٰهِ اِنَّهٗ رَبِّیۡۤ اَحۡسَنَ مَثۡوَایَ ؕ اِنَّهٗ لَا یُفۡلِحُ الظّٰلِمُوۡنَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: মাআজাল্লাহি ইন্নাহু রব্বী আহসানা মাছওয়া-ইয়া, ইন্নাহু লা ইউফলিহুজ জোয়ালিমূন
dua.bangla_translation: [I seek] the refuge of Allah. Indeed, he is my master, who has made good my residence. Indeed, wrongdoers will not succeed.
dua.footnote: Prophet Yusuf (AS) sought refuge in Allah in this way at the moment the wife of Al-Aziz of Egypt tried to seduce him

dua.title: Dua When Boarding a Ship
dua.dua_in_arabic: بِسۡمِ اللّٰهِ مَ‍‍جۡؔرٖىهَا وَ مُرۡسٰىهَا ؕ اِنَّ رَبِّیۡ لَغَفُوۡرٌ رَّحِیۡمٌ
dua.dua_in_bangla: বিসমিল্লাহি মাজরিহা ওয়া মুরসাহা, ইন্না রব্বি লা গফুরুর রহীম
dua.bangla_translation: In the name of Allah is its course and its anchorage. Indeed, my Lord is Forgiving and Merciful.
dua.footnote: Prophet Nuh's (AS) dua when boarding the ship

dua.title: Dua for a City's Safety and Provision for Its People
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ اجۡعَلۡ هٰذَا بَلَدًا اٰمِنًا وَّ ارۡزُقۡ اَهۡلَهٗ مِنَ الثَّمَرٰتِ مَنۡ اٰمَنَ مِنۡهُمۡ بِاللّٰهِ وَ الۡیَوۡمِ الۡاٰخِرِ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বিজ আল হাযা বালাদান আমিনাও অরযুক আহলাহু মিনাছ ছামারতি মান আমানা মিনহুম বিল্লাহি অল ইয়াওমিল আখির
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, make this a secure city and provide its people with fruits - whoever of them believes in Allah and the Last Day.
dua.footnote: Prophet Ibrahim's (AS) dua for the city of Makkah and its inhabitants

dua.title: Dua to Be Numbered Among the Righteous
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبَّنَا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَكَفِّرْ عَنَّا سَيِّئَاتِنَا وَتَوَفَّنَا مَعَ الْأَبْرَارِ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বানা ফাগফির লানা যুনূবানা ওয়া কাফফির আন্না ছায়্যিআতিনা ওয়া তাওয়াফফানা মাআল আবরার
dua.bangla_translation: Our Lord, forgive us our sins and remove from us our misdeeds, and cause us to die with the righteous.

dua.title: Dua to Protect Oneself and Family from Punishment - 1
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ اجۡعَلۡ هٰذَا الۡبَلَدَ اٰمِنًا وَّ اجۡنُبۡنِیۡ وَ بَنِیَّ اَنۡ نَّعۡبُدَ الۡاَصۡنَامَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বিজ আল হাযাল বালাদা আমিনাও ওয়াজনুবনী ওয়া বানিয়্যা আন নাআবুদা আছনাম
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, make this city secure, and keep me and my sons away from worshipping idols.
dua.footnote: Prophet Ibrahim's (AS) dua to protect himself and his children from idol worship

dua.title: Dua to Protect Oneself and Family from Punishment - 2
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ نَجِّنِیۡ وَ اَهۡلِیۡ مِمَّا یَعۡمَلُوۡنَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বি নাজ্জিনী ওয়া আহলি মিম্মা ইআমালুন
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, save me and my family from [the consequence of] what they do.
dua.footnote: Prophet Lut's (AS) dua to Allah to save himself and his family from the consequences of his people's wrongdoing

dua.title: Dua for Patience and to Die as a Muslim
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبَّنَا أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَتَوَفَّنَا مُسْلِمِينَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বানা আফরিগ আলাইনা ছবরাও ওয়া তাওয়াফফানা মুসলিমীন।
dua.bangla_translation: Our Lord, pour upon us patience and let us die as Muslims [in submission to You].
dua.footnote: The magicians employed by Pharaoh made this prayer to Allah after embracing Islam upon witnessing the miracle of Prophet Musa (AS)

dua.title: Dua for Good in This World and the Hereafter
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বানা আতিনা ফিদ্ দুনইয়া হাসানাতাও ওয়া ফিল আখিরাতি হাসানাতাও ওয়া ক্বিনা আযাবান নার
dua.bangla_translation: Our Lord, give us good in this world and good in the Hereafter and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.

dua.title: Dua for Relief from Suffering and Illness
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَنِّیۡ مَسَّنِیَ الضُّرُّ وَ اَنۡتَ اَرۡحَمُ الرّٰحِمِیۡنَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আন্নী মাসসানিয়াদ্ দুররু ওয়া আনতা আরহামুর রহিমীন
dua.bangla_translation: Indeed, adversity has touched me, and You are the Most Merciful of the merciful.
dua.footnote: Prophet Ayyub's (AS) dua to Allah for relief from suffering and illness. Allah accepted his prayer and granted him health and wealth.

dua.title: Dua for Increase in Knowledge
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ زِدْنِي عِلْمًا
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বি যিদনী ইলমা
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, increase me in knowledge.
dua.footnote: Allah instructed the Prophet (peace be upon him) to make this dua, so he would seek increase in knowledge rather than rush to memorize the revelation

dua.title: Dua for the Light of Paradise
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبَّنَا أَتْمِمْ لَنَا نُورَنَا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا إِنَّكَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বানা আতমিম লানা নূরানা ওয়াগফির লানা ইন্নাকা আলা কুল্লি শাইয়িন ক্বাদীর
dua.bangla_translation: Our Lord, perfect for us our light and forgive us. Indeed, You are over all things competent.
dua.footnote: The believers will make this prayer on the Day of Judgement, seeking Allah's help to attain their final reward, Paradise

dua.title: Dua for Deliverance from Oppressors
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ نَجِّنِي مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বি নাজ্জিনী মিনাল ক্বওমিয যালিমীন
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, save me from the wrongdoing people.
dua.footnote: Prophet Musa's (AS) plea to Allah to save him from the oppressive people of Pharaoh

dua.title: Dua for Deliverance from the Terrible Punishment of Hell
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبَّنَا اصْرِفْ عَنَّا عَذَابَ جَهَنَّمَ إِنَّ عَذَابَهَا كَانَ غَرَامًا. إِنَّهَا سَاءَتْ مُسْتَقَرًّا وَمُقَامًا
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বানছ রিফ আন্না আযাবা জাহান্নামা ইন্না আযাবাহা কানা গারামা, ইন্না ছাআত মুছতাকাররাও ওয়া মুকামা
dua.bangla_translation: Our Lord, avert from us the punishment of Hell. Indeed, its punishment is ever adhering; indeed, it is evil as a settlement and residence.

dua.title: Dua of the Oppressed Seeking a Helper Against Oppressors
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبَّنَاۤ اَخۡرِجۡنَا مِنۡ هٰذِهِ الۡقَرۡیَۃِ الظَّالِمِ اَهۡلُهَا ۚ وَ اجۡعَلۡ لَّنَا مِنۡ لَّدُنۡکَ وَلِیًّا ۚۙ وَّ اجۡعَلۡ لَّنَا مِنۡ لَّدُنۡکَ نَصِیۡرًا
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বানাখ রিজনা মিন হাজিহিল ক্বরেইয়াতিজ জ্বলিমিন আহলিহা, ওয়াজআলনা মিললাদুনকা ওয়ালিইয়্যাও ওয়াজল লানা মিললাদুনকা নাছীরা
dua.bangla_translation: Our Lord, take us out of this city of oppressive people and appoint for us from Yourself a protector and appoint for us from Yourself a helper.

dua.title: Dua for Deliverance from the Punishment of Hell
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبَّنَا مَا خَلَقْتَ هَذَا بَاطِلًا سُبْحَانَكَ فَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বানা মা খলাকতা হাযা বাত্বিলান ছুবহানাকা ফাক্বিনা আযাবান নার
dua.bangla_translation: Our Lord, You did not create this [i.e. the heavens and earth] without purpose; exalted are You [above such a thing]. Then protect us from the punishment of the Fire.

dua.title: Prayer for Refuge Against Oppressors and Corrupters
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ انْصُرْنِي عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْمُفْسِدِينَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বিনছুরনী আলাল ক্বাওমিল মুফসিদীন
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, support me against the corrupting people.
dua.footnote: Prophet Lut's (AS) dua seeking help against his people's wrongdoing

dua.title: Dua to Be Delivered from a Wrongdoing Nation and to Save the Believers
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ اِنَّ قَوۡمِیۡ کَذَّبُوۡنِ.ۖ فَافۡتَحۡ بَیۡنِیۡ وَ بَیۡنَهُمۡ فَتۡحًا وَّ نَجِّنِیۡ وَ مَنۡ مَّعِیَ مِنَ الۡمُؤۡمِنِیۡنَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বী ইন্না ক্বওমী কাজ্জাবূন। ফাফতাহ বাইনী ওয়া বাইনাহুম ফাতহাও ওয়া নাজ্জিনী ওয়ামাম মায়িইয়া মিনাল মুমিনুন।
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, my people have denied me. So decide between me and them a clear decision, and save me and those with me of the believers.
dua.footnote: Prophet Nuh's (AS) prayer to Allah after his people threatened to stone him

dua.title: Prayer for Refuge from Ignorance
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَعُوۡذُ بِاللّٰهِ اَنۡ اَکُوۡنَ مِنَ الۡجٰهِلِیۡنَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আউযুবিল্লাহি আন আকূনা মিনাল জাহিলীন
dua.bangla_translation: I seek refuge in Allah from being among the ignorant.

dua.title: Dua Not to Be Among the Wrongdoing People
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ فَلَا تَجۡعَلۡنِیۡ فِی الۡقَوۡمِ الظّٰلِمِیۡنَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বি ফালা তাজআলনী ফিল ক্বওমিজ জোয়ালিমীন।
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, then do not place me among the wrongdoing people.

dua.title: Dua for Spouses and Children Who Are a Comfort to the Eyes
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبَّنَا هَبْ لَنَا مِنْ أَزْوَاجِنَا وَذُرِّيَّاتِنَا قُرَّةَ أَعْيُنٍ وَاجْعَلْنَا لِلْمُتَّقِينَ إِمَامًا
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বানা হাব লানা মিন আযওয়াজিনা ওয়া যুররিয়্যাতিনা ক্বুররাতা আইউনিও ওয়াজআলনা লিলমুত্তাকিনা ইমামা।
dua.bangla_translation: Our Lord, grant us from among our wives and offspring comfort to our eyes and make us leaders for the righteous.

dua.title: Prayer for Forgiveness and Mercy
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبَّنَا آمَنَّا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَا وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الرَّاحِمِينَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বানা আমান্না ফাগফির লানা ওয়ারহামনা ওয়া আনতা খাইরুর রাহহিমীন।
dua.bangla_translation: Our Lord, we have believed, so forgive us and have mercy upon us, and You are the best of the merciful.

dua.title: Dua Before Eating
dua.dua_in_arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ
dua.dua_in_bangla: বিসমিল্লা-হ
dua.bangla_translation: In the name of Allah.

dua.title: If I Forget to Say 'Bismillah'
dua.dua_in_arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ اَوَّلَهٗ وَاٰخِرَهٗ
dua.dua_in_bangla: বিসমিল্লা-হি আওয়ালাহু ওয়া আ-খিরহ্
dua.bangla_translation: In the name of Allah, at the beginning and the end, in every circumstance.

dua.title: To Recite After Finishing Eating
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ الَّذِيْ اَطْعَمَنِيْ هٰذَا وَرَزَقَنِيْهِ مِنْ غَيْرِ حَوْلٍ مِّنِّيْ وَلَا قُوَّةٍ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আল’হামদু লিল্লা-হিল্লাযী আত’আমানী হা-যা ওয়া রাঝাক্বানীহি মিন গাইরি ‘হাওলিম মিন্নী ওয়ালা ক্বুওয়্যাহ
dua.bangla_translation: All praise is due to Allah, who fed me this food and provided it for me, without any power or ability on my part.
dua.benefitorhadith: The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said, "Whoever eats food and then says this will have his past sins forgiven."

dua.title: To Recite After Drinking Milk
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِيْهِ وَزِدْنَا مِنْهُ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আল্লা-হুম্মা বা-রিক লানা- ফীহি ওয়া যিদনা মিনহু
dua.bangla_translation: O Allah, bless it for us and provide us with more of it.

dua.title: Dua to Recite After Eating at Someone's Invitation
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ اَطْعِمْ مَنْ اَطْعَمَنِيْ وَاسْقِ مَنْ سَقَانِيْ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আল্লা-হুম্মা, আত্ব’ইম মান আত্ব'আমানী, ওয়াসক্বি মান সাক্বা-নী
dua.bangla_translation: O Allah, feed the one who has fed me, and give drink to the one who has given me drink.

dua.title: Dua to Say to the Host After Eating at an Invitation
dua.dua_in_arabic: أَكَلَ طَعَامَكُمُ الْأَبْرَارُ، وَصَلَّتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ، وَأَفْطَرَ عِنْدَكُمُ الصَّائِمُونَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আকালা ত্বোয়ামাকুমুল আবরার ওয়াছল্লাত আলাইকুমুল মালায়িকাহ্‌ ওয়া আফ্‌ তার ইন্দাকুমুছ ছ’য়িমুন
dua.bangla_translation: May Allah cause righteous people to eat your food, may the angels invoke blessings on you, and may those who are fasting break their fast at your home.

dua.title: To Say When Meeting a Muslim
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَلسَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَةُ اللّٰهِ وَبَرَكَاتُهُ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আস-সালা-মু ‘আলাইকুম ওয়া রা’হমাতুল্লা-হি ওয়া বারাকা-তুহ
dua.bangla_translation: Peace, mercy, and blessings of Allah be upon you.

dua.title: To Say in Reply to Salam
dua.dua_in_arabic: وَعَلَيْكُمُ السَّلَامُ وَرَحْمَةُ اللهِ وَبَرَكَاتُهٗ
dua.dua_in_bangla: ওয়া আলাইকুমুস্‌ সালাম ওয়ারাহ্‌মাতুল্লাহি ওয়াবারাকাতুহ।
dua.bangla_translation: And upon you be peace, and the mercy and blessings of Allah.

dua.title: Reply When a Non-Muslim Gives Salam
dua.dua_in_arabic: وَعَلَيْكُمْ
dua.dua_in_bangla: ওয়া ‘আলাইকুম
dua.bangla_translation: And upon you too.

dua.title: Dua to Recite Before Sleeping
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ بِاسْمِكَ اَمُوْتُ وَاَحْيَا
dua.dua_in_bangla: আল্লাাহুম্মা বিসমিকা আমূতু ওয়া আহইয়াা।
dua.bangla_translation: O Allah, I will die in Your name and live in Your name.

dua.title: To Recite Upon Waking
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ الَّذِيْ اَحْيَانَا بَعْدَ مَا اَمَاتَنَا وَ اِلَيْهِ النُّشُوْرُ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আলহামদু লিল্লাা হিল্লাযী আহ ইয়াানাা বা‘দা মাা আমাা তানাা ওয়া ইলাইহিন নুশূর।
dua.bangla_translation: All praise is due to Allah, who gave us life after having caused us to die, and to Him is the return.

dua.title: To Recite Before Entering the Toilet
dua.dua_in_arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ، اَللّٰهُمَّ اِنِّيْ اَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْخُبُثِ وَالْخَبَائِثِ
dua.dua_in_bangla: বিসমিল্লা-হ, আল্লা-হুম্মা, ইন্নী আ‘উযু বিকা মিনাল খুবুছি ওয়াল খাবা-ইছ
dua.bangla_translation: In the name of Allah. O Allah, I seek refuge in You from male and female devils.

dua.title: To Recite After Leaving the Toilet
dua.dua_in_arabic: غُفْرَانَكَ اَ لْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ الَّذِيْ اَذْهَبَ عَنِّيْ الْاَذٰى وَعَافَانِيْ
dua.dua_in_bangla: গুফরাানাকা আলহামদু লিল্লাা হিল্লাযী আযহাবা ‘আন্নীল আযাা ওয়া ‘আা ফাা নী।
dua.bangla_translation: O Allah, I seek Your forgiveness. All praise is due to the Great Allah, who removed harm from me and kept me in good health.

dua.title: To Recite at the Start of Wudu
dua.dua_in_arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ
dua.dua_in_bangla: বিসমিল্লা-হ
dua.bangla_translation: In the name of Allah.

dua.title: To Recite During Wudu
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِيْ ذَنبِـيْ وَوَسِّعْ لِـيْ فِيْ دَارِيْ وَبَارِكْ لِيْ فِيْ رِزْقِيْ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আল্লাহুম্মাগফিরলি যাম্বি ওয়া ওয়াসসি’লি ফি দারি ওয়া বারিকলি ফি রিযকি।
dua.bangla_translation: O Allah, forgive my sins. Widen my dwelling. Increase my provision.

dua.title: To Recite After Wudu
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَشْهَدُ اَنْ لَّاۤ اِلٰهَ اِلَّا اللهُ وَحْدَهٗ لَا شَرِيْكَ لَهٗ. وَاَشْهَدُ اَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا عَبْدُهٗ وَرَسُوْلُهٗ. اَللّٰهُمَّ اجْعَلْنِيْ مِنَ التَوَّابِيْنَ. وَاجْعَلْنِـيْ مِنَ الْمُتَطَهِّرِيْنَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আশাহাদু আল্ লা ইলাহা ইল্লাল্লাহু ওয়াহ্‌দাহু লা শারিকালাহ্‌ ওয়া আশ্‌হাদু আন্না মুহাম্মাদান্‌ আ’বদুহু ওয়া রাসুলুহ্‌, আল্লাহুম্মা জা’আলনি মিনাত্তাওয়াবিনা ওয়াজা’আলনি মিনাল মুতাত্বাহ্‌হিরিন।
dua.bangla_translation: I bear witness that there is no god but Allah alone, without any partner, and I bear witness that Muhammad ﷺ is His servant and Messenger. O Allah, make me among those who repent and among those who purify themselves.

dua.title: To Recite After Drinking Water
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ الَّذِي سَقَانَا مَاءً عَذْبًا فُرَاتًا بِرَحْمَتِهِ، وَلَمْ يَجْعَلْهُ مِلْحًا أُجَاجًا بِذُنُوبِنَا
dua.dua_in_bangla: আলহাম্‌দু লিল্লাহিল্লাজি ছাকানা আজবান ফুরতান বিরহমাতিহ্‌ ওয়ালাম ইয়াজ্‌ আ’লহু মিলহান উজাজান বিযুনুবিনা।
dua.bangla_translation: All praise is due to Allah, who by His mercy gave us sweet, pleasant water to drink and did not make it bitter and salty because of our sins.

dua.title: To Say After Sneezing
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আল-‘হামদু লিল্লা-হ
dua.bangla_translation: All praise is due to Allah.

dua.title: To Say to the One Who Sneezed
dua.dua_in_arabic: يَرْحَمُكَ اللّٰهُ
dua.dua_in_bangla: ইয়ার‘হামুকাল্লা-হ
dua.bangla_translation: May Allah have mercy on you.

dua.title: The Sneezer Then Replies
dua.dua_in_arabic: يَهْدِيكُمُ اللّٰهُ وَيُصْلِحُ بَالَكُمْ
dua.dua_in_bangla: ইয়াহ্‌ দিকুমুল্লহু ওইয়ুছ লিহু বালাকুম
dua.bangla_translation: May Allah guide you and set your affairs right.

dua.title: Upon Entering the Mosque
dua.dua_in_arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ. وَالصَّلَاةُ وَالسَّلَامُ عَلٰى رَسُوْلِ اللّٰهِ. اَللّٰهُمَّ افْتَحْ لِيْ اَبْوَابَ رَحْمَتِكَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: বিসমিল্লাহি ওয়াস্‌সালাতু ওয়াস্‌সালামু’ আলা রাসুলিল্লাহ্‌ আল্লাহুম্মাফ্‌তাহলি আবওয়াবা রাহমাতিক্‌।
dua.bangla_translation: I enter in the name of Allah. May peace and blessings be upon the Messenger of Allah. O Allah, open for me the doors of Your mercy.

dua.title: Upon Leaving the Mosque
dua.dua_in_arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ. وَالصَّلَاةُ وَالسَّلَامُ عَلٰى رَسُوْلِ اللّٰهِ. اَللّٰهُمَّ اِنِّيْ اَسْئَلُكَ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ.
dua.dua_in_bangla: বিসমিল্লাহি ওয়াস্‌সালাতু ওয়াস্‌সালামু আলা রাসুলিল্লাহ্‌ আল্লাহুমা ইন্নি আস’আলুকা মিন ফাদ্‌লিক।
dua.bangla_translation: I begin in the name of Allah. May blessings, mercy, and peace be upon the Messenger of Allah. O Allah, I ask You for Your bounty [i.e. lawful provision].

dua.title: Dua for Entering the Mosque (at least once daily)
dua.dua_in_arabic: ‏ أَعُوْذُ بِاللّٰهِ الْعَظِيْمِ وَبِوَجْهِهِ الْكَرِيْمِ وَسُلْطَانِهِ الْقَدِيْمِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيْمِ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আ‘ঊযু বিল্লা-হিল ‘আযীম, ওয়া বিওয়াজহিহিল কারীম, ওয়াসুলতা-নিহিল ক্বদীম, মিনাশ শাইত্বা-নির রাজীম
dua.bangla_translation: I seek refuge in Allah, the Almighty, in His noble Face, and in His eternal authority, from the accursed Satan.

dua.title: Dua for Leaving the House
dua.dua_in_arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى اللّٰهِ لَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ اِلَّا بِاللّٰهِ
dua.dua_in_bangla: বিসমিল্লা-হি, তাওয়াক্কালতু ‘আলাল্লা-হি, লা- 'হাওলা ওয়ালা-ক্বুওয়্যাতা ইল্লা- বিল্লাহ।
dua.bangla_translation: In the name of Allah, I place my trust in Allah. There is no power and no strength except with Allah.
dua.benefitorhadith: Narrated by Anas (RA): The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said, "Whoever says this upon leaving his house will be told: You are guided, sufficed, and protected. And Satan moves away from him." In another narration: one devil says to another, "How can we harm a man who has been guided, sufficed, and protected?"

dua.title: Dua for Entering the House
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَللّٰهُمَّ اِنِّيْ اَسْاَلُكَ خَيْرَ الْمَوْلِجِ وَخَيْرَ الْمَخْرَجِ بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ وَلَجْنَا وَبِسْمِ اللّٰهِ خَرَجْنَا وَعَلَى اللّٰهِ رَبِّنَا تَوَكَّلْنَا
dua.dua_in_bangla: আল্লা-হুম্মা, ইন্নী- আস্‌আলুকা খাইরাল মাউলিজি ওয়া খাইরাল মাখ্‌রাজি, বিসমিল্লা-হি ওয়ালাজনা- ওয়া বিসমিল্লা-হি খারাজনা- ওয়া ‘আলাল্লাহি রাব্বিনা- তাওয়াক্কালনা-
dua.bangla_translation: O Allah, I ask You for the best of entrances and the best of exits. In the name of Allah we enter, and in the name of Allah we leave, and upon Allah, our Lord, we rely.
dua.benefitorhadith: The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) commanded remembrance of Allah upon entering the house, and said that if a house is entered with the remembrance of Allah, Satan cannot stay there. The essential sunnah remembrance upon entering the house is the greeting of Salam. Narrated by Abu Malik Al-Ash'ari (RA): The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said, "When one of you enters, let him say this, then give salam to his family."

dua.title: Dua When Wearing Clothes
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ الَّذِيْ كَسَانِيْ هَذَا الثَّوْبَ وَرَزَقَنِيْهِ مِنْ غَيْرِ حَوْلٍ مِّنِّيْ وَلَا قُوَّةٍ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আল’হামদু লিল্লা-হিল্লাযী কাসা-নী হা-যাছছাওবা ওয়া রাযাক্বানীহি মিন গাইরি ‘হাওলিম মিন্নী ওয়ালা- ক্বুওয়্যাহ
dua.bangla_translation: Praise be to Allah, who has clothed me with this garment and provided it for me, without any power or ability on my part.
dua.benefitorhadith: The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said, "Whoever says this while putting on clothes will have his past sins forgiven."

dua.title: Dua When Wearing New Clothes
dua.dua_in_arabic: ٖاَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ الَّذِيْ كَسَانِىْ مَا اُوَارِيْ بِهٖ عَوْرَتِـيْ وَاَتَجَمَّلُ بِهٖ فِيْ حَيَاتِيْ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আলহামদু লিল্লাা হিল্লাযী কাসাানী মাা উ ওয়াারী বিহী ‘আউরতী ওয়া আতা জামমালু বিহী ফী হায়াাতী।
dua.bangla_translation: All praise is due to Allah, who has clothed me with this garment, by which I cover my private parts and adorn myself in my life.

dua.title: Dua When Riding a Vehicle
dua.dua_in_arabic: سُبْحَانَ الَّذِيْ سَخَّرَ لَنَا هٰذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهٗ مُقْرِنِيْنَ. وَاِنَّا اِلٰى رَبِّنَا لَمُنْقَلِبُوْنَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: সুবহাানাল্লাযী সাখখরা লানাা হাা যাা ওয়ামাা কুন্নাা লাহূ মুক্ব রিনীন ওয়া ইন্নাা ইলাা রব্বিনাা লামুন ক্বলিবূন।
dua.bangla_translation: Glory be to Him who has subjected this to us, and we could not have [otherwise] subdued it, and indeed we, to our Lord, will [surely] return [after death].

dua.title: Dua When Boarding a Boat or Floating Vessel
dua.dua_in_arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ مَجْرَاهَا وَمُرْسٰىهَا اِنَّ رَبِّىْ لَغَفُوْرٌ رَّحِيْمٌ
dua.dua_in_bangla: বিসমিল্লা-হি মাজ্‌রাহা- ওয়া মুর্‌সা-হা- ইন্না- রব্বী লাগাফূরুর রহীম
dua.bangla_translation: In the name of Allah is its course and its anchorage. Indeed, my Lord is Forgiving and Merciful.
dua.benefitorhadith: Prophet Nuh (AS) recited the following dua when boarding the ship -

dua.title: To Recite When Visiting a Sick Person
dua.dua_in_arabic: لاَ بَأْسَ طَهُوْرٌ إِنْ شَآءَ اللّٰهُ  أَسْأَلُ اللّٰهَ الْعَظِيْمَ رَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيْمِ أَنْ يَّشْفِيَكَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: লা বা’সা তাহুরুন ইন-শা-আল্লাহ >> আস আলুল্লাাহাল ‘আযীম রব্বাল ‘আরশিল ‘আযীম আইঁ ইয়াশ ফিয়াক।
dua.bangla_translation: No harm, [it will be] purification [of outer and inner impurity], Allah willing. Then recite this seven times: I ask the Mighty Allah, Lord of the Mighty Throne, to cure you.

dua.title: To Recite When Leaving a Gathering
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِيْ، وَتُبْ عَلَيَّ، إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ التَّوَّابُ الغَفُوْرُ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বিগফির লী ওয়াতুব ‘আলাইয়্যা, ইন্নাকা আনতাত্ তাউওয়া-বুল গাফূর
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, forgive me and accept my repentance; indeed You are the Accepter of repentance, the Forgiving.

dua.title: Dua to Suppress Anger
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَعُوْذُ بِاللّٰهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيْمِ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আ‘ঊযুবিল্লাাহি মিনাশ শাইতাানির রজীম।
dua.bangla_translation: I seek refuge in Allah from the accursed Satan.

dua.title: Dua When Looking in a Mirror
dua.dua_in_arabic: اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ اَللّٰهُمَّ كَمَا حَسَّنْتَ خَلْقِيْ فَحَسِّنْ خُلُقِيْ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আলহামদু লিল্লাাহি আল্লাাহুম্মা কামাা হাসসানতা খলক্বী ফাহাসসিন খুলুক্বী।
dua.bangla_translation: All praise is due to Allah. O Allah, just as You have made my appearance good, make my character good as well.

dua.title: Surah Al-Fatiha for Any Illness
dua.dua_in_arabic:
بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ
الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ 
الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ 
مَالِكِ يَوْمِ الدِّينِ 
إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ وَإِيَّاكَ نَسْتَعِينُ 
اهْدِنَا الصِّرَاطَ الْمُسْتَقِيمَ 
صِرَاطَ الَّذِينَ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ غَيْرِ الْمَغْضُوبِ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا الضَّالِّينَ
dua.reference: The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said, "Surah Al-Fatiha is a great remedy for every illness." (Sunan Ad-Darimi, Hadith 3413; Mishkat, Hadith 2170)
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (RA): Some Companions passed by an Arab tribe. The tribe did not host them. Suddenly the tribe's leader was bitten by a snake. They came and asked, "Do you have any medicine?" The Companions said, "Yes, but you must host us; we are very hungry." The tribe agreed to host them and also offered a flock of goats. One Companion recited Umm al-Quran (Surah Al-Fatiha), gathered saliva in his mouth, and applied it to the wound. The poison subsided and the man was cured. The Companions ate and returned to the Prophet (peace be upon him) with the goats, asking whether their action was proper. He smiled and said, "It is fine; take the goats, and set aside a share for me too." (Bukhari, Hadith 5006)

Ja'far As-Sadiq (RA) narrated that reciting Surah Al-Fatiha 40 times, blowing over water, and sprinkling it on the face of a feverish person will, by its blessing, remove the fever.

Reciting this surah 41 times between the sunnah and fard of Fajr, then blowing on the eyes, relieves eye pain.

dua.title: Dua for a Sick Person's Recovery
dua.dua_in_arabic: أَسْأَلُ اللّٰهَ الْعَظِيْمَ رَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيْمِ أَنْ يَّشْفِيَكَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আসআলুল্লা-হাল 'আযীম, রাব্বাল 'আরশিল 'আযীম আ'ইয়াশফিইয়াক
dua.bangla_translation: I ask the Mighty Allah, Lord of the Mighty Throne, to cure you.
dua.benefitorhadith: Narrated by Ibn Abbas (RA): The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said, "If any Muslim visits a sick person whose time [of death] has not yet come and recites this seven times, Allah will cure him."

dua.title: Dua to Recite with Each Verse of Surah Al-Fatiha for Any Illness
dua.dua_in_arabic:
أعُوْذُ بِاللّٰهِ السَّمِيْعِ الْعَلِيْمِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيْمِ مِنْ هَمْزِهِ وَنَفْخِهِ وَنَفْتِهِ

بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيْمِ

الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ( أَسْأَلُ اللّٰهَ الْعَظِيْمَ رَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ أَنْ يَّشْفِيَكَ).

الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيْمِ ( أَسْأَلُ اللّٰهَ الْعَظِيْمَ رَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ أَنْ يَّشْفِيَكَ).

مٰلِكِ يَوْمِ الدِّيْنِ ( أَسْأَلُ اللّٰهَ الْعَظِيْمَ رَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ أَنْ يَّشْفِيَكَ).

إِيَّاكَ نَعْبُدُ وَإِيَّاكَ نَسْتَعِيْنُ ( أَسْأَلُ اللّٰهَ الْعَظِيْمَ رَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ أَنْ يَّشْفِيَكَ).

إِهْدِنَا الصِّرَاطَ الْمُسْتَقِيْمَ ( أَسْأَلُ اللّٰهَ الْعَظِيْمَ رَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ أَنْ يَّشْفِيَكَ).

صِرَاطَ الَّذِيْنَ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ ( أَسْأَلُ اللّٰهَ الْعَظِيْمَ رَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ أَنْ يَّشْفِيَكَ).

غَيْرِ الْمَغْضُوْبِ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا الضَّالِّيْنَ ( أَسْأَلُ اللّٰهَ الْعَظِيْمَ رَبَّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ أَنْ يَّشْفِيَكَ).
dua.rules: First recite the ruqyah "A'udhu billah", then the ordinary "Bismillah", then recite the dua below once after each verse of Surah Al-Fatiha.
dua.benefitorhadith: First recite the ruqyah "A'udhu billah", then the ordinary "Bismillah", then recite the dua below after each verse of Surah Al-Fatiha. Surah Al-Fatiha has 7 verses in total, and this dua is also mentioned to be recited 7 times. Surah Al-Fatiha is itself a healing, and this particular dua is also well known for healing. Reciting the two together greatly increases the effectiveness of the ruqyah.

dua.title: Dua for Pain
dua.dua_in_arabic:
بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ

أَعُوذُ بِاللّٰهِ بِعِزَّةِ اللّٰهِ وَقُدْرَتِهِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَجِدُ وَأَحَاذِرُ
dua.dua_in_bangla:
বিসমিল্লাহ (তিনবার)
আ'উযু বিল্লা-হি (আঊযু বি'ইয্যাতিল্লা-হি) ওয়া কুদরাতিহী মিন শারির মা- আজিদু ওয়া উ'হা-যির (৭ বার)
dua.bangla_translation: In the name of Allah (three times). I seek refuge in Allah's might and power from the evil of what I feel and am wary of (seven times).
dua.rules: Dua for pain: say Bismillah three times, then recite the dua below seven times.
dua.footnote: In one narration this dua appears without the phrase "bi 'izzatillah" (by Allah's might); in another it appears with it. So we may recite it either with or without this phrase, Allah willing.
dua.benefitorhadith: Uthman ibn Abi al-As (RA) told the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) about a pain in his body. The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said, "Place your hand on the place where you feel pain and say Bismillah three times, then say this dua seven times."

dua.title: Dua for Boils, Lumps, etc.
