dua.dua_in_arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ ، تُرْبَةُ أَرْضِنَا بِرِيْقَةِ بَعْضِنَا يُشْفَى سَقِيمُنَا بِإِذْنِ رَبَّنَا
dua.dua_in_bangla: বিসমিল্লা-হি। তুরবাতু আরদ্বিনা-, বিরীক্বতি বা'অদ্বিনা-,ইউশফা সাক্বীমুনা, বিইযনি রাব্বিনা-
dua.bangla_translation: With the name of Allah, mixing the dust of our earth with the saliva of some of us, our sick one will be cured, by our Lord's leave.
dua.rules: First take a little saliva on the index finger of the right hand, place the finger on the ground, then recite the dua below, and apply it to the affected area.
dua.footnote: Since it's hard to find natural soil in Dhaka city, placing the finger on potted soil should also work if unavailable, Allah willing.
dua.benefitorhadith: Narrated by Aisha (RA): When someone had an ailment, pain, or wound, the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) would place his index finger on the ground, lift it up, and recite this dua. In another narration, he would apply a little of his own saliva to his finger, place it on the ground, then place it on the site of pain while saying this dua.

dua.title: Dua for Recovery from Illness 1
dua.dua_in_arabic: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ النَّاسِ أَذْهِبِ الْبَأْسَ اِشْفِ وَأَنْتَ الشَّافِيْ ، لَا شِفَاءَ إِلَّا شِفَآؤُكَ شِفَآءً لَّا يُغَادِرُ سَقَمًا
dua.dua_in_bangla: আল্লা-হুম্মা রাব্বান না-স, আহিবিল বা-'স, ইল্ফি, ওয়া আনতাশ শা-ফী, লা- শিফা-আ ইল্লা-শিফা-উকা, শিফা-আন লা- ইউগা-দিরু সাক্বামা
dua.bangla_translation: O Allah, Lord of mankind, remove the affliction and grant healing, for You are the Healer; there is no healing except Your healing, a healing that leaves behind no illness.
dua.rules: Place your right hand on the sick person's body and recite the dua below.
dua.benefitorhadith: Narrated by Aisha (RA): When one of us fell ill, the Prophet (peace be upon him) would rub his right hand over the patient's body and say this dua.

dua.title: Dua for Recovery from Illness 2
dua.dua_in_arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ أُرْقِيَكَ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ يُؤْذِيْكَ مِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ نَفْسٍ أَوْ عَيْنِ حَاسِدٍ، اَللّٰهُ يَشْفِيْكَ، بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ أَرْقِيكَ
dua.dua_in_bangla: বিসমিল্লা-হি আক্বীক, মিন কুল্লি শাইয়িন ইউ'যীক, মিন শারি কুল্লি নাফসিন আউ আ'ইনিন 'হা-সিদিন, আল্লা-হু ইয়াশফীক, বিসমিল্লা-হি আক্বীক
dua.bangla_translation: In the name of Allah I perform ruqyah for you, from everything that troubles you, from the evil of every soul or envious eye, may Allah cure you. In the name of Allah I perform ruqyah for you.
dua.rules: Recite this dua when visiting a sick person.
dua.benefitorhadith: Narrated by Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (RA): Jibreel (AS) came to the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) while he was suffering from fever. Jibreel (AS) said, "Muhammad, are you ill?" He said, "Yes." Then Jibreel (AS) recited the above dua.

dua.title: Treatment for the Evil Eye
dua.dua_in_arabic: أعُوْذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللّٰهِ التَّآمَّةِ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْطَانٍ وَّهَآمَّةٍ، وَمِنْ كُلِّ عَيْنٍ لَّآمَّةٍ
dua.dua_in_bangla: আ'উযু বিকালিমা-তিল্লা-হিত তা-ম্মাতি মিন কুল্লি শাইত্বা-নিওঁ ওয়া হা-ম্মাহ, ওয়া মিন কুল্লি 'আইনিল লা-ম্মাহ
dua.bangla_translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah, from every devil and poisonous creature, and from every evil, harmful eye.
dua.rules: For treatment of the evil eye, recite the dua below three times.
dua.benefitorhadith: The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) used to protect Hasan (RA) and Husain (RA) with these words. He said that Ibrahim (AS) used to protect his two sons Isma'il and Ishaq (AS) with these words.

dua.title: Dua for Recovery from Illness 3
dua.dua_in_arabic: بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ يُبْرِيْكَ، وَمِنْ كُلِّ دَاءٍ يَّشْفِيْكَ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ، وَشَرِّ كُلِّ ذِيْ عَيْنٍ
dua.dua_in_bangla: বিসমিল্লাহি ইউবরিকা ওয়া মিন কুল্লি দা-ইন ইয়াশফিকা ওয়া মিন শাররি হা-সিদিন ইযা হাসাদ, ওয়া শাররি কুল্লি যি 'আইন
dua.bangla_translation: In the name of Allah, may He heal you, from every illness, and from the evil of an envier when he envies, and from the harm of every evil eye.
dua.rules: Recite this dua three times over the sick person and blow on them.
dua.benefitorhadith: Narrated by Ibn Abi Umar Al-Makki (RA), reported from Aisha (RA): when the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) fell ill, Jibreel (AS) would recite this dua and blow on him.

dua.title: Dua to Drive Away Satan from Children's Anger, Stubbornness, and Crying
dua.dua_in_arabic: رَبِّ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ هَمَزَاتِ الشَّيَاطِيْنِ، وَأَعُوْذُ بِكَ رَبِّ أَنْ يَّحْضُرُوْنِ
dua.dua_in_bangla: রব্বি আ'উযুবিকা মিন্ হামাযা-তিশ্ শাইয়া-জ্বীন, ওয়া'আ'উযুবিকা রব্বি আঁই ইয়াহ্ দুরূন্
dua.bangla_translation: My Lord, I seek refuge in You from the incitements of the devils, and I seek refuge in You, my Lord, lest they be present with me.
dua.rules: To drive away anger, stubbornness, crying, and Satan among children, recite the dua below three times.
dua.benefitorhadith:
Narrated by Abu Hurairah (RA): I heard the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) say, "There is no child born except that Satan touches him at the time of birth, and he cries out from the touch of Satan, except Maryam and her son [Isa, AS]." Abu Hurairah then said, "O Allah, indeed I seek refuge with You for him and his offspring from the accursed Satan."

Sahih al-Bukhari 3431

dua.title: To Remove Fear
dua.dua_in_arabic:
(1)  أَعُوْذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللّٰهِ التَّآمَّةِ مِنْ غَضَبِهِ وَعِقَابِهِ وَشَرِّ عِبَادِهِ، وَمِنْ هَمَزَاتِ الشَّيَاطِيْنِ وَأَنْ يَّحْضُرُوْنِ

(2)  قُلْنَا لَا تَخَفْ إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْأَعْلٰ
dua.dua_in_bangla:
১। আ'ঊযু বিকালিমা-তিল্লাহিত তাম্মা-তি মিন 'গাদ্বাবিহী ওয়া 'ইক্কাবিহী ওয়া শাররি 'ইবাদিহী, ওয়া মিন হামাঝা-তিশ শায়া-তীনি ওয়া আইয়া'হদুরুন।

২। ক্বুলন্না- লা-তাখফ ইন্নাকা আংতাল আ'লা-।
dua.bangla_translation:
1. I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah, from His wrath and punishment, from the evil of His creation, and from the incitements of the devils and their presence with me.

2. Do not fear, you will be victorious.
dua.rules: If frightened during sleep, recite the dua below. Or recite the second dua while placing a hand on the chest, seven times.
dua.benefitorhadith: Narrated by Amr ibn Shu'aib (RA), from his father and grandfather: The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said that when one of you is frightened during sleep, let him recite this dua.

dua.title: 6 Verses of Healing (Ayat Ash-Shifa) for Recovery from Illness
dua.dua_in_arabic:
(1)  وَيَشْفِ صُدُوْرَ قَوْمٍ مُّؤْمِنِيْنَ
 (2) وَشِفَاءٌ لِّمَا فِي الصُّدُوْرِ وَهُدًى وَّرَحْمَةٌ لِّلْمُؤْمِنِيْنَ
(3)  يَخْرُجُ مِنْ بُطُوْنِهَا شَرَابٌ مُّخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوَانُهُ فِيْهِ شِفَآءٌ لِّلنَّاسِ 
(4) وَنُنَزِّلُ مِنَ الْقُرْءَانِ مَا هُوَ شِفَآءٌ وَّرَحْمَةٌ لِّلْمُؤْمِنِيْنَ
(5)  وَإِذَا مَرِضْتُ فَهُوَ يَشْفِيْنِ
(6)  قُلْ هُوَ لِلَّذِيْنَ اٰمَنُوْا هُدًى وَشِفَآءٌ
dua.dua_in_bangla:
১। ওয়া ইয়াশফি ছুদু-রা ক্বাওমিম মু'মিনি-ন।
২। ওয়া শিফাউ'ল লিমা- ফিচ্ছুদু-রি ওয়া হুদাও ওয়া রহমাতুল লিল মু'মিনি-ন।
৩। ইয়াখরুঝু মিমবুতু-নিহা- শারা-বুম মুখতালিফুন, আলওয়ানুহু-ফি-হি শিফা-উ লিন্না-সি।
৪। ওয়া নুনাজ্জিলু মিনাল কুরআ'নি মা হুয়া শিফাউও ওয়া রাহমাতুল লিলমু'মিনি-ন।
৫। ওয়া ইজা মারিদতু ফা হুয়া ইয়াশফি-নি।
৬। কুল হুওয়া লিল্লাযীনা আ-মানু হুদাওঁ ওয়া শিফা-উন
dua.bangla_translation:
1. And Allah brings peace to the hearts of the believers.
2. And it is a healing for what is in the hearts, and guidance and mercy for the believers.
3. There emerges from their bellies a drink of varying colors, in which there is healing for people.
4. And We send down of the Quran that which is a healing and a mercy for the believers.
5. And when I am ill, it is He who cures me.
6. Say, "It is, for those who believe, a guidance and a healing."
dua.reference:
1. Surah At-Tawbah, Ayah 14
2. Surah Yunus, Ayah 57
3. Surah An-Nahl, Ayah 69
4. Surah Bani Isra'il, Ayah 82
5. Surah Ash-Shu'ara, Ayah 80
6. Surah Ha-Mim (Fussilat), Ayah 44
dua.rules: Six verses of the Noble Quran for healing from illness. It is best to recite Surah Al-Fatiha before these.

dua.benefitorhadith: benefitorhadith

hadith.hadis: Except one who comes to Allah with a sound heart.
My Lord, grant me judgement and join me with the righteous.
Those who believe fight in the cause of Allah, and those who disbelieve fight in the cause of Taghut. So fight against the allies of Satan; indeed, the plot of Satan has ever been weak.
O you who have believed, if you support Allah, He will support you and plant firmly your feet.
Narrated by Anas (RA): The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) forbade drinking water while standing.

Narrated by Buraydah al-Aslami (RA): The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "Whoever gives respite to a person in hardship will have a reward of charity for every day, and whoever extends it beyond the due date will have a reward equal to charity for every day."
Narrated by Abdullah ibn Mughaffal (RA): The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said, "Indeed Allah is gentle and loves gentleness; He gives for gentleness what He does not give for harshness."

Narrated by Abu Hurairah (RA): The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said, "The most complete of the believers in faith are those with the best character, and the best of you are those who are best to their wives."
And whatever strikes you of disaster is for what your hands have earned; but He pardons much.

Whoever fears Allah, He will make for him a way out, and will provide for him from where he does not expect. And whoever relies upon Allah, He is sufficient for him. Indeed, Allah will accomplish His purpose. Allah has already set for everything a due measure.
